  ```typescript
    attributes: Array<{ trait_type: string; value: string }>;
    floorPriceUsd: number | null;
  }
  
  async function getMetaplexNFTPortfolio(walletAddress: string): Promise<NFTAsset[]> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const resp = await client.NftService.getNftsForAddress(
      'solana-mainnet',
      walletAddress,
      { noSpam: true }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', resp.error_message);
      return [];
    }
  
    const collections = resp.data?.items ?? [];
    const assets: NFTAsset[] = [];
  
    for (const collection of collections) {
      for (const nft of collection.nft_data ?? []) {
        assets.push({
          tokenId: nft.token_id ?? '',
          name: nft.external_data?.name ?? collection.contract_name ?? 'Unknown NFT',
          imageUrl: nft.external_data?.image ?? '',
          description: (nft.external_data?.description as string) ?? '',
          collectionName: collection.contract_name ?? '',
          collectionAddress: collection.contract_address ?? '',
          attributes:
            (nft.external_data?.attributes as Array<{ trait_type: string; value: string }>) ?? [],
          floorPriceUsd: collection.floor_price_quote ?? null,
        });
      }
    }
  
    console.log(`Loaded ${assets.length} NFTs across ${collections.length} collections`);
    return assets;
  }
  
  // Usage
  getMetaplexNFTPortfolio('7Np41oeYqPefeAfUZx9j5yCLrMQZMDyApbJAKhgQFMRm').then((nfts) => {
    nfts.slice(0, 5).forEach((n) =>
      console.log(`${n.name} | ${n.collectionName} | Floor: $${n.floorPriceUsd ?? 'N/A'}`)
    );
  });
  ```

**multichain-nft-gallery**
  Description: Discover and display every NFT a wallet holds across all chains in one gallery — scan with getAddressActivity then fetch NFTs per active chain.
  Use case: Build a cross-chain NFT gallery that shows all NFTs a wallet holds on every chain — Ethereum PFPs, Base collections, Polygon gaming assets — in a single responsive grid.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_nft/
  Cost: 0.5/call (activity) + 1/chain (NFTs)
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +2 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to discover NFTs for across all chains)
  Response fields: chain, items[].contract_name, items[].contract_address, items[].nft_data[].token_id, items[].nft_data[].external_data.name, items[].nft_data[].external_data.image, items[].nft_data[].external_data.attributes[].trait_type, items[].nft_data[].external_data.attributes[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/multichain-nft-gallery
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  const EVM_CHAINS = new Set([
    'eth-mainnet','base-mainnet','matic-mainnet','bsc-mainnet',
    'optimism-mainnet','arbitrum-mainnet','avalanche-mainnet',
    'gnosis-mainnet','blast-mainnet','linea-mainnet',
  ]);
  
  async function fetchMultichainNFTs(walletAddress: string) {
    // 1. Discover active chains
    const activityResp = await client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress);
    const activeChains = (activityResp.data?.items ?? [])
      .filter(c => !c.is_testnet && EVM_CHAINS.has(c.name ?? ''))
      .map(c => c.name!);
  
    if (activeChains.length === 0) return [];
  
    // 2. Fetch NFTs on all active chains in parallel
    const nftResults = await Promise.allSettled(
      activeChains.map(chain =>
        client.NftService.getNftsForAddress(chain, walletAddress)
          .then(r => ({ chain, items: r.data?.items ?? [] }))
      )
    );
  
    // 3. Flatten and collect all NFTs
    const allNfts = nftResults
      .filter((r): r is PromiseFulfilledResult<any> => r.status === 'fulfilled')
      .flatMap(r => r.value.items.flatMap((col: any) =>
        (col.nft_data ?? []).map((nft: any) => ({
          chain: r.value.chain,
          collection: col.contract_name,
          contractAddress: col.contract_address,
          tokenId: nft.token_id,
          name: nft.external_data?.name,
          image: nft.external_data?.image,
          attributes: nft.external_data?.attributes ?? [],
        }))
      ));
  
    return allNfts;
  }
  ```

**nft-collection-analytics**
  Description: Holder count, top holders, transfer velocity, and ownership concentration for any ERC-721 collection — build analytics dashboards without a marketplace API.
  Use case: Build an NFT collection insights page showing unique holder count, top 10 whale holders, daily transfer volume, and holder concentration — all from on-chain data.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/tokens/{collectionContract}/token_holders_v2/, GET /v1/{chainName}/events/topics/{topicHash}/?sender-address={collectionContract}
  Cost: 0.02/holder + 0.01/event
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, gnosis
  Key params: chainName (Foundational chain only — eth-mainnet, base-mainnet, bsc-mainnet, matic-mainnet, optimism-mainnet, gnosis-mainnet), collectionContract (NFT collection contract address to analyze), pageSize (Number of holders per page — max 100)
  Response fields: items[].address, items[].balance, items[].total_supply, items[].balance_percent_of_total, transferCount24h, uniqueHolders
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-collection-analytics
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  const TRANSFER_TOPIC = '0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef';
  
  async function getNftCollectionAnalytics(chainName: string, collectionContract: string) {
    // 1. Fetch top holders page
    const holdersResp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenHoldersV2ForTokenAddress(
      chainName, collectionContract, { pageNumber: 0, pageSize: 100 }
    );
    const holders = holdersResp.data?.items ?? [];
  
    // 2. Compute concentration — top 10 holders % of supply
    const top10 = holders.slice(0, 10);
    const top10Pct = top10.reduce((s, h) => s + (h.balance_percent_of_total ?? 0), 0);
    const totalSupply = holders[0]?.total_supply ?? '0';
    const uniqueHolders = holders.length;
  
    // 3. Fetch recent Transfer events for 24h volume
    const transferResp = await client.BaseService.getLogEventsByTopicHash(
      chainName, TRANSFER_TOPIC
    );
    const oneDayAgo = Date.now() - 86_400_000;
    const recentTransfers = (transferResp.data?.items ?? []).filter(e => {
      const sender = e.sender_address?.toLowerCase();
      const eventTime = new Date(e.block_signed_at ?? 0).getTime();
      return sender === collectionContract.toLowerCase() && eventTime > oneDayAgo;
    });
  
    return {
      uniqueHolders,
      totalSupply,
      top10HoldersPct: top10Pct.toFixed(2),
      transferCount24h: recentTransfers.length,
      topHolders: top10.map(h => ({
        address: h.address,
        balance: h.balance,
        pctOfSupply: h.balance_percent_of_total,
      })),
    };
  }
  ```

**nft-gallery-viewer**
  Description: Drop-in React component that renders a full NFT gallery for any wallet using GoldRush Kit's NFTWalletCollectionView — zero custom UI needed.
  Use case: Add a fully styled NFT gallery to any React/Next.js app in under 10 lines — no custom UI required, just drop in the GoldRush Kit component.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_nft/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier), walletAddress (0x wallet address whose NFTs to display), apikey (GoldRush API key passed to GoldRushProvider)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_address, items[].nft_data[].token_id, items[].nft_data[].token_url, items[].nft_data[].external_data.name, items[].nft_data[].external_data.image
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-gallery-viewer
  ```typescript
  // Install: npm install @covalenthq/goldrush-kit
  import { GoldRushProvider, NFTWalletCollectionView } from '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit';
  import '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit/styles.css';
  
  export function NftGallery({ walletAddress }: { walletAddress: string }) {
    return (
      <GoldRushProvider apikey={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!}>
        <NFTWalletCollectionView
          address={walletAddress}
          chain_names={['eth-mainnet', 'base-mainnet', 'matic-mainnet']}
        />
      </GoldRushProvider>
    );
  }
  ```

**nft-metadata-fetcher**
  Description: Retrieve full NFT metadata, traits, and ownership history for any token onchain.
  Use case: Build an NFT detail page that shows token image, traits, description, and owner for any NFT in a wallet — the foundation for any NFT explorer.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_nft/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_address, items[].nft_data[].token_id, items[].nft_data[].token_url, items[].nft_data[].external_data.name, items[].nft_data[].external_data.description, items[].nft_data[].external_data.image, items[].nft_data[].external_data.attributes[].trait_type, items[].nft_data[].external_data.attributes[].value, items[].nft_data[].owner
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-metadata-fetcher
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getNftMetadata(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.NftService.getNftsForAddress(chainName, walletAddress);
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items.flatMap(collection =>
      (collection.nft_data ?? []).map(nft => ({
        collection: collection.contract_name,
        contractAddress: collection.contract_address,
        tokenId: nft.token_id,
        name: nft.external_data?.name,
        image: nft.external_data?.image,
        description: nft.external_data?.description,
        traits: nft.external_data?.attributes ?? [],
        owner: nft.owner,
      }))
    );
  }
  ```

**nft-portfolio-valuation**
  Description: Value an entire NFT portfolio using live collection and market data.
  Use case: Build a wallet NFT portfolio page showing every collection held, total NFT count per collection, and token thumbnails across all supported chains.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_nft/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, matic-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_address, items[].nft_data[].token_id, items[].nft_data[].external_data.name, items[].nft_data[].external_data.image, items[].nft_data[].owner
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-portfolio-valuation
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getNftPortfolio(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.NftService.getNftsForAddress(chainName, walletAddress);
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items.map(collection => ({
      collection: collection.contract_name,
      contractAddress: collection.contract_address,
      count: (collection.nft_data ?? []).length,
      tokens: (collection.nft_data ?? []).map(nft => ({
        tokenId: nft.token_id,
        name: nft.external_data?.name,
        image: nft.external_data?.image,
      })),
    }));
  }
  ```

**nft-sale-history**
  Description: Full transfer and sale history for a single NFT token ID — who owned it, when it sold, and for how much. Powers provenance viewers and NFT detail pages.
  Use case: Build an NFT provenance page showing every historical owner, transfer timestamp, and inferred sale price from ERC-20 payments — ideal for NFT detail pages and rarity tools.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_nft/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transfers_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address holding or previously holding the NFT), contractAddress (NFT collection contract address)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].nft_data[].token_id, items[].nft_data[].owner, items[].transfers[].from_address, items[].transfers[].to_address, items[].transfers[].delta, items[].transfers[].block_signed_at, items[].transfers[].tx_hash
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-sale-history
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getNftSaleHistory(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const [nftResp, txResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.NftService.getNftsForAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.BalanceService.getErc20TransfersForWalletAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
    ]);
  
    if (nftResp.error) throw new Error(nftResp.error_message);
    if (txResp.error) throw new Error(txResp.error_message);
  
    const nfts = nftResp.data.items.flatMap(c =>
      (c.nft_data ?? []).map(n => ({ collection: c.contract_name, tokenId: n.token_id, owner: n.owner }))
    );
  
    const transfers = txResp.data.items.flatMap(i => i.transfers ?? []);
  
    return { nfts, recentTransfers: transfers.slice(0, 20) };
  }
  ```

**nft-traits-explorer**
  Description: Fetch all trait types and their value distributions for an NFT collection. Essential for rarity calculators, collection analytics, and trait-based filtering UIs.
  Use case: Build a trait rarity calculator that shows how rare each attribute combination is across a collection — the core of any NFT analytics platform.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_nft/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, matic-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address holding NFTs from the collection)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_address, items[].nft_data[].token_id, items[].nft_data[].external_data.attributes[].trait_type, items[].nft_data[].external_data.attributes[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-traits-explorer
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function exploreNftTraits(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.NftService.getNftsForAddress(chainName, walletAddress);
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const traitCounts: Record<string, Record<string, number>> = {};
  
    for (const collection of resp.data.items) {
      for (const nft of collection.nft_data ?? []) {
        for (const attr of nft.external_data?.attributes ?? []) {
          const { trait_type, value } = attr;
          if (!trait_type || !value) continue;
          traitCounts[trait_type] ??= {};
          traitCounts[trait_type][String(value)] =
            (traitCounts[trait_type][String(value)] ?? 0) + 1;
        }
      }
    }
  
    return traitCounts;
  }
  ```

**nft-transfer-monitor**
  Description: Real-time feed of NFT mints, sales, and transfers for any collection — subscribe to ERC-721 Transfer events via the wallet activity stream.
  Use case: Build a live collection activity feed that shows every mint, transfer, and sale for an NFT collection as it happens — like OpenSea's activity tab but in real-time via WebSocket.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/events/topics/{topicHash}/?sender-address={collectionContract}
  Cost: 0.01/event
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, blast
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain where the NFT collection is deployed), collectionContract (NFT collection contract address to monitor for Transfer events), topicHash (ERC-721 Transfer topic — 0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef)
  Response fields: items[].block_signed_at, items[].block_height, items[].tx_hash, items[].decoded.params[].name, items[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-transfer-monitor
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  const TRANSFER_TOPIC = '0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef';
  
  async function pollNftTransfers(
    chainName: string,
    collectionContract: string,
    onTransfer: (event: any) => void,
    intervalMs = 12_000
  ) {
    let lastSeenBlock = 0;
  
    async function poll() {
      try {
        const resp = await client.BaseService.getLogEventsByTopicHash(
          chainName, TRANSFER_TOPIC,
          { startingBlock: lastSeenBlock > 0 ? lastSeenBlock + 1 : undefined }
        );
        const events = (resp.data?.items ?? [])
          .filter(e => e.sender_address?.toLowerCase() === collectionContract.toLowerCase());
  
        for (const event of events) {
          const params = event.decoded?.params ?? [];
          const from = params.find((p: any) => p.name === 'from')?.value ?? '';
          const to = params.find((p: any) => p.name === 'to')?.value ?? '';
          const tokenId = params.find((p: any) => p.name === 'tokenId')?.value ?? '';
          const isMint = from === '0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000';
  
          onTransfer({
            type: isMint ? 'MINT' : 'TRANSFER',
            from, to, tokenId,
            txHash: event.tx_hash,
            blockTime: event.block_signed_at,
            blockHeight: event.block_height,
          });
  
          if ((event.block_height ?? 0) > lastSeenBlock) {
            lastSeenBlock = event.block_height!;
          }
        }
      } catch (err) {
        console.error('Poll error:', err);
      }
    }
  
    await poll();
    return setInterval(poll, intervalMs);
  }
  ```

**nft-transfer-tracker**
  Description: Monitor NFT mints, transfers, and burns as they happen onchain.
  Use case: Build an NFT transfer notification system that alerts users when NFTs enter or leave their wallet — mint detector, transfer feed, and burn tracker in one.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transfers_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, matic-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address to track NFT movements for)
  Response fields: items[].transfers[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].transfers[].from_address, items[].transfers[].to_address, items[].transfers[].delta, items[].transfers[].block_signed_at, items[].transfers[].tx_hash
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-transfer-tracker
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  const ZERO = '0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000';
  
  async function trackNftTransfers(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getErc20TransfersForWalletAddress(
      chainName,
      walletAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items
      .flatMap(item => item.transfers ?? [])
      .map(t => ({
        type: t.from_address === ZERO ? 'mint'
            : t.to_address === ZERO ? 'burn'
            : 'transfer',
        symbol: t.contract_ticker_symbol,
        from: t.from_address,
        to: t.to_address,
        txHash: t.tx_hash,
        timestamp: t.block_signed_at,
      }));
  }
  ```

**nft-gated-app**
  Description: Complete Next.js boilerplate with wallet connect, SIWE authentication, and NFT ownership gating — deploy a token-gated product in under an hour.
  Use case: Ship a token-gated product (Discord alternative, exclusive content site, holder dashboard) where access requires owning any NFT in a specific collection.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/tokens/{collectionContract}/nft_check/{walletAddress}/
  Cost: 1 credit/check
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, arbitrum, optimism, bsc
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain where the NFT collection is deployed), walletAddress (Wallet address of the user requesting access), collectionContract (NFT collection contract address to check ownership against)
  Response fields: data.is_owner
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-gated-app
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  // pages/api/auth/nft-gate.ts — Next.js API route
  export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
    try {
      const { walletAddress, signature, message } = await req.json();
  
      // 1. Verify SIWE message signature (use siwe package in production)
      if (!walletAddress || !signature) {
        return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Missing wallet or signature' }, { status: 400 });
      }
  
      const chainName = process.env.NFT_CHAIN ?? 'eth-mainnet';
      const collectionContract = process.env.NFT_CONTRACT!;
  
      // 2. Check NFT ownership via GoldRush
      const resp = await client.NftService.checkOwnershipInNft(
        chainName, walletAddress, collectionContract
      );
  
      const isOwner = resp.data?.is_owner ?? false;
  
      if (!isOwner) {
        return NextResponse.json(
          { isOwner: false, error: 'You do not hold a token from this collection.' },
          { status: 403 }
        );
      }
  
      // 3. Issue a session cookie or JWT here — return success
      return NextResponse.json({
        isOwner: true,
        redirect: '/dashboard',
        wallet: walletAddress,
      });
    } catch (err: any) {
      return NextResponse.json({ error: err.message }, { status: 500 });
    }
  }
  ```

**ronin-nft-inventory**
  Description: List all NFTs held in a Ronin wallet — Axie Infinity, Land, and other Sky Mavis NFT collections.
  Use case: Build a Ronin wallet NFT viewer showing all Axie Infinity, Land, and Sky Mavis NFT collections held by any Ronin address — the foundation for Axie portfolio apps.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/axie-mainnet/address/{walletAddress}/balances_nft/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ronin
  Key params: walletAddress (Ronin wallet address (0x format or ronin: prefix stripped))
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_address, items[].nft_data[].token_id, items[].nft_data[].external_data.name, items[].nft_data[].external_data.image, items[].nft_data[].external_data.attributes[]
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/ronin-nft-inventory
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getRoninNftInventory(walletAddress: string) {
    // Always use 'axie-mainnet' for Ronin chain
    const resp = await client.NftService.getNftsForAddress(
      'axie-mainnet',
      walletAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items.map(collection => ({
      collectionName: collection.contract_name,
      contractAddress: collection.contract_address,
      nftCount: (collection.nft_data ?? []).length,
      nfts: (collection.nft_data ?? []).map(nft => ({
        tokenId: nft.token_id,
        name: nft.external_data?.name,
        image: nft.external_data?.image,
        traits: nft.external_data?.attributes ?? [],
      })),
    }));
  }
  ```

**solana-nft-collection-stats**
  Description: Fetch aggregated on-chain stats for any Solana NFT collection by collection address — floor price in SOL and USD, 24h trading volume, total unique holder count, total listed supply, and top 5 holder wallets ranked by number of items. Pair with GoldRush's real-time NFT transfer stream to track when floor listings are scooped. Build a collection monitor that fires an alert when floor drops below your target price, volume spikes above 2x average, or holder concentration increases.
  Use case: Build a Solana NFT collection monitor that tracks floor price, 24h volume, and holder concentration and fires alerts when key thresholds are crossed.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/nfts/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: solana
  Key params: tokenAddress (The collection mint address or verified collection address to fetch stats for), address (Wallet address to check for NFTs from this collection), page_size (Number of top holders to return for concentration analysis), no_spam (Filter out spam NFT collections from holder queries)
  Response fields: items[].address, items[].balance, items[].total_supply, items[].contract_name, items[].contract_address, pagination.total_count, items[].nft_data[].token_id, items[].floor_price_quote, items[].contract_ticker_symbol
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-nft-collection-stats
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface NFTCollectionStats {
    collectionAddress: string;
    collectionName: string;
    totalHolders: number;
    totalSupply: number;
    floorPriceUsd: number | null;
    top5Holders: Array<{ wallet: string; itemCount: number }>;
    holderConcentrationPct: number;
  }
  
  async function getNFTCollectionStats(
    collectionAddress: string
  ): Promise<NFTCollectionStats | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const holdersResp = await client.BaseService.getTokenHoldersV2(
      'solana-mainnet',
      collectionAddress,
      { pageSize: 10 }
    );
  
    if (holdersResp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', holdersResp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const holders = holdersResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const totalHolders = holdersResp.data?.pagination?.total_count ?? 0;
    const totalSupply = Number(holders[0]?.total_supply ?? 0);
  
    const top5Holders = holders.slice(0, 5).map((h) => ({
      wallet: h.address ?? '',
      itemCount: Number(h.balance ?? 0),
    }));
  
    const top5Balance = top5Holders.reduce((sum, h) => sum + h.itemCount, 0);
    const holderConcentrationPct =
      totalSupply > 0 ? parseFloat(((top5Balance / totalSupply) * 100).toFixed(2)) : 0;
  
    return {
      collectionAddress,
      collectionName: holders[0]?.contract_name ?? 'Unknown Collection',
      totalHolders,
      totalSupply,
      floorPriceUsd: null,
      top5Holders,
      holderConcentrationPct,
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  getNFTCollectionStats('J1S9H3QjnRtBbbuD4HjPV6RpRhwuk4zKbxsnCHuTgh9w').then((stats) => {
    if (stats) {
      console.log(`${stats.collectionName}: ${stats.totalHolders} holders`);
      console.log(`Top 5 control ${stats.holderConcentrationPct}% of supply`);
    }
  });
  ```

### Analytics (16 skills)

**block-explorer-toolkit**
  Description: Fetch block details, all transactions in a block, and decode individual transactions — all the building blocks for a custom block explorer.
  Use case: Build a custom block explorer that shows block details, gas utilization, and a decoded transaction list — replacing Etherscan for your specific chain or dApp.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/block_v2/{blockHeight}/, GET /v1/{chainName}/{txHash}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), blockHeight (Block number or 'latest'), txHash (Transaction hash to decode)
  Response fields: data.height, data.signed_at, data.tx_count, data.gas_used, data.gas_limit, items[].tx_hash, items[].from_address, items[].to_address, items[].value, items[].fees_paid, items[].successful
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/block-explorer-toolkit
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function exploreBlock(chainName: string, blockHeight: string) {
    const blockResp = await client.BaseService.getBlock(chainName, blockHeight);
  
    if (blockResp.error) throw new Error(blockResp.error_message);
  
    const block = blockResp.data;
    return {
      height: block.height,
      timestamp: block.signed_at,
      txCount: block.tx_count,
      gasUsed: block.gas_used,
      gasLimit: block.gas_limit,
      utilization: block.gas_limit
        ? ((block.gas_used ?? 0) / block.gas_limit * 100).toFixed(1) + '%'
        : 'N/A',
    };
  }
  ```

**contract-event-dashboard**
  Description: Build a real-time event monitor for any smart contract — poll decoded event logs and display a live activity feed for any protocol or token.
  Use case: Build a live protocol dashboard that shows every decoded event emitted by a smart contract — e.g. Swap, Mint, Transfer, Approval — in a real-time activity feed.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/events/address/{contractAddress}/
  Cost: 0.01/event
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain where the contract is deployed), contractAddress (Smart contract address to monitor for decoded events), startingBlock (Block height to start scanning from), endingBlock (Block height to stop scanning at), pageNumber (0-indexed page for paginating through events)
  Response fields: items[].block_signed_at, items[].block_height, items[].tx_hash, items[].decoded.name, items[].decoded.params[].name, items[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/contract-event-dashboard
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function startContractEventDashboard(
    chainName: string,
    contractAddress: string,
    onEvent: (event: any) => void,
    intervalMs = 12_000
  ) {
    let lastSeenBlock = 0;
  
    async function poll() {
      try {
        const resp = await client.BaseService.getLogEventsByAddress(
          chainName, contractAddress,
          { startingBlock: lastSeenBlock > 0 ? lastSeenBlock + 1 : undefined }
        );
        const events = resp.data?.items ?? [];
  
        for (const event of events) {
          if ((event.block_height ?? 0) <= lastSeenBlock) continue;
  
          const decoded = {
            eventName: event.decoded?.name ?? 'Unknown',
            params: Object.fromEntries(
              (event.decoded?.params ?? []).map((p: any) => [p.name, p.value])
            ),
            txHash: event.tx_hash,
            blockHeight: event.block_height,
            blockTime: event.block_signed_at,
          };
  
          onEvent(decoded);
  
          if ((event.block_height ?? 0) > lastSeenBlock) {
            lastSeenBlock = event.block_height!;
          }
        }
      } catch (err) {
        console.error('Poll error:', err);
      }
    }
  
    await poll(); // immediate first fetch
    return setInterval(poll, intervalMs);
  }
  ```

**contract-event-log-monitor**
  Description: Stream all decoded event logs emitted by any smart contract — filter by topic hash, date, or block range. The foundation for DEX analytics, protocol dashboards, and real-time monitoring.
  Use case: Build a protocol activity dashboard that shows every event emitted by a contract — Deposit, Withdraw, Swap — with decoded parameters and timestamps.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/events/address/{contractAddress}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), contractAddress (Smart contract address to monitor), startingBlock (Start block height for the query range), endingBlock (End block height (or 'latest'))
  Response fields: items[].block_signed_at, items[].tx_hash, items[].decoded.name, items[].decoded.params[].name, items[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/contract-event-log-monitor
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function monitorContractEvents(
    chainName: string,
    contractAddress: string,
    startingBlock: number,
    endingBlock: number
  ) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getLogEventsByAddress(
      chainName,
      contractAddress,
      { startingBlock, endingBlock }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items.map(log => ({
      event: log.decoded?.name,
      txHash: log.tx_hash,
      timestamp: log.block_signed_at,
      params: Object.fromEntries(
        (log.decoded?.params ?? []).map(p => [p.name, p.value])
      ),
    }));
  }
  ```

**cross-contract-event-scanner**
  Description: Scan ALL contracts on a chain for a specific event by topic hash — e.g. find every ERC-20 Transfer or every Uniswap Swap across the entire chain.
  Use case: Find every Uniswap V3 Swap event on Base within the last 1000 blocks — build cross-protocol DEX aggregators, airdrop eligibility scanners, and chain-wide analytics.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/events/topics/{topicHash}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), topicHash (keccak256 hash of the event signature (e.g. Transfer(address,address,uint256))), startingBlock (Start block height), endingBlock (End block height)
  Response fields: items[].block_signed_at, items[].tx_hash, items[].sender_address, items[].decoded.name, items[].decoded.params[].name, items[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/cross-contract-event-scanner
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  // Transfer(address indexed from, address indexed to, uint256 value)
  const TRANSFER_TOPIC = '0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef';
  
  async function scanTransferEvents(
    chainName: string,
    startingBlock: number,
    endingBlock: number
  ) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getLogEventsByTopicHash(
      chainName,
      TRANSFER_TOPIC,
      { startingBlock, endingBlock }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items.map(log => ({
      contract: log.sender_address,
      txHash: log.tx_hash,
      timestamp: log.block_signed_at,
      params: Object.fromEntries(
        (log.decoded?.params ?? []).map(p => [p.name, p.value])
      ),
    }));
  }
  ```

**event-log-decoder**
  Description: Decode and search contract event logs across any block range and chain.
  Use case: Build a contract debugging tool that shows every event emitted by a contract with fully decoded parameters — from, to, amount, value — in a readable format.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/events/address/{contractAddress}/, GET /v1/{chainName}/block/{blockHeight}/log_events/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), contractAddress (Contract to decode logs for), blockHeight (Block height or range to query logs from), topics (Array of topic hashes to filter)
  Response fields: items[].block_signed_at, items[].tx_hash, items[].decoded.name, items[].decoded.params[].name, items[].decoded.params[].value, items[].decoded.params[].type
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/event-log-decoder
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function decodeEventLogs(
    chainName: string,
    contractAddress: string,
    startingBlock: number,
    endingBlock: number
  ) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getLogEventsByAddress(
      chainName,
      contractAddress,
      { startingBlock, endingBlock }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items
      .filter(log => log.decoded)
      .map(log => ({
        event: log.decoded!.name,
        txHash: log.tx_hash,
        timestamp: log.block_signed_at,
        args: Object.fromEntries(
          (log.decoded!.params ?? []).map(p => [p.name, p.value])
        ),
      }));
  }
  ```

**gas-analytics**
  Description: Real-time and historical gas price tracking for any EVM-compatible chain.
  Use case: Build a gas fee estimator widget that shows real-time slow/fast/instant gas prices in gwei and estimated USD cost for a standard transaction.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/event/{eventType}/gas_prices/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, matic-mainnet)), eventType (erc20 | nativetokens | uniswapv3)
  Response fields: items[].base_fee, items[].open.gas_price, items[].open.fee_usd, items[].fast.gas_price, items[].fast.fee_usd, items[].instant.gas_price, items[].instant.fee_usd
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/gas-analytics
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getGasPrices(chainName: string) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getGasPrices(chainName, 'erc20');
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const latest = resp.data.items[0];
    return {
      baseFeeGwei: latest.base_fee,
      slow: {
        gasPriceGwei: latest.open?.gas_price,
        estimatedUsd: latest.open?.fee_usd,
      },
      fast: {
        gasPriceGwei: latest.fast?.gas_price,
        estimatedUsd: latest.fast?.fee_usd,
      },
      instant: {
        gasPriceGwei: latest.instant?.gas_price,
        estimatedUsd: latest.instant?.fee_usd,
      },
    };
  }
  ```

**institutional-wallet-tracker**
  Description: Monitor large-wallet movements and institutional-grade position changes across chains.
  Use case: Build a whale watcher dashboard that tracks top wallet positions across chains — flag large buys, sells, and new positions as they happen.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_v3/
  Cost: 1 credit/call per wallet
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x address of the whale or institutional wallet to track)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].logo_url
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/institutional-wallet-tracker
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  const WHALE_WALLETS = [
    '0x28c6c06298d514db089934071355e5743bf21d60', // Binance hot wallet
    '0x47ac0fb4f2d84898e4d9e7b4dab3c24507a6d503', // Jump Trading
  ];
  
  async function trackWhalePositions(chainName: string) {
    const results = await Promise.all(
      WHALE_WALLETS.map(async (addr) => {
        const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(
          chainName, addr
        );
        if (resp.error) return null;
        const totalUsd = resp.data.items.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0);
        return { address: addr, totalUsd, topHoldings: resp.data.items.slice(0, 5) };
      })
    );
    return results.filter(Boolean);
  }
  ```

**onchain-data-query**
  Description: Query indexed blockchain data across 100+ chains with a single unified API.
  Use case: Build a wallet analytics dashboard that combines token balances and recent transactions into a single unified view — the entry point for any onchain data product.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_v3/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].successful
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/onchain-data-query
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function queryWalletData(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const [balResp, txResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(chainName, walletAddress, 0),
    ]);
  
    if (balResp.error) throw new Error(balResp.error_message);
    if (txResp.error) throw new Error(txResp.error_message);
  
    return {
      balances: balResp.data.items.filter(t => !t.is_spam),
      recentTxs: txResp.data.items.slice(0, 10),
    };
  }
  ```

**smart-money-copier**
  Description: Track known alpha wallets and surface their new token buys the moment they execute — build a copy trading radar using wallet activity streaming.
  Use case: Build a copy trading intelligence tool that monitors a curated list of smart money wallets and surfaces new token buys in real-time.
  Endpoints: wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql — walletTxs subscription, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/
  Cost: Streaming + 1 credit/balance check
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, arbitrum, optimism
  Key params: walletAddresses (Curated list of smart money wallet addresses to monitor), chainName (EVM chain to monitor for copy trades), minPositionUsd (Minimum USD value of a new buy to surface as a signal)
  Response fields: wallet, txHash, tokenSymbol, tokenAddress, deltaAmount, estimatedUsdValue
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/smart-money-copier
  ```typescript
  import { createClient } from 'graphql-ws';
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const wsClient = createClient({
    url: 'wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql',
    connectionParams: { apiKey: process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY },
  });
  const apiClient = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  const KNOWN_WALLETS = [
    '0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045', // vitalik.eth (example)
  ];
  
  function startSmartMoneyCopier(
    chainName: string,
    minPositionUsd: number,
    onSignal: (signal: any) => void
  ) {
    KNOWN_WALLETS.forEach(walletAddress => {
      wsClient.subscribe(
        {
          query: `subscription($addr: String!, $chain: String!) {
            walletTxs(walletAddress: $addr, chainName: $chain) {
              txHash blockSignedAt transfers { tokenSymbol tokenAddress delta fromAddress }
            }
          }`,
          variables: { addr: walletAddress, chain: chainName },
        },
        {
          next: async ({ data }: any) => {
            const tx = data?.walletTxs;
            if (!tx) return;
            // Find incoming token transfers (buys)
            const buys = (tx.transfers ?? []).filter(
              (t: any) => t.fromAddress?.toLowerCase() !== walletAddress.toLowerCase()
            );
            for (const buy of buys) {
              // Confirm position size by fetching current balance
              try {
                const balResp = await apiClient.BalanceService
                  .getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(chainName, walletAddress);
                const position = (balResp.data?.items ?? []).find(
                  t => t.contract_address?.toLowerCase() === buy.tokenAddress?.toLowerCase()
                );
                const estimatedUsdValue = position?.quote ?? 0;
                if (estimatedUsdValue >= minPositionUsd) {
                  onSignal({
                    wallet: walletAddress, txHash: tx.txHash,
                    tokenSymbol: buy.tokenSymbol, tokenAddress: buy.tokenAddress,
                    deltaAmount: buy.delta, estimatedUsdValue,
                  });
                }
              } catch (_) {}
            }
          },
          error: console.error,
          complete: () => {},
        }
      );
    });
  }
  ```

**solana-program-activity-monitor**
  Description: Track the on-chain growth of any Solana program — DEX, lending protocol, game, or DePIN — using GoldRush's transaction data on `solana-mainnet`. Fetch daily instruction call count, unique signing wallets per day, week-over-week growth rate, and cumulative user count over any 90-day window. Compare two programs head-to-head on the same metrics. Use for investor due diligence on early-stage protocols, health monitoring dashboards, or surfacing underrated programs with breakout growth before they trend.
  Use case: Build a Solana protocol analytics dashboard that tracks daily active users, instruction volume, and week-over-week growth for any program to surface breakout protocols.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (The Solana program address to monitor for activity metrics), page_size (Number of transactions to fetch per page for aggregation (max 100)), page (Page index for paginating through 90-day transaction history), block_signed_at_asc (Set to true to retrieve oldest-first for chronological aggregation)
  Response fields: items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].successful, items[].from_address, items[].fees_paid, items[].log_events[].sender_name, items[].log_events[].decoded.name, pagination.total_count, pagination.page_number
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-program-activity-monitor
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface DailyMetric {
    date: string;
    txCount: number;
    uniqueWallets: number;
  }
  
  interface ProgramActivityReport {
    programAddress: string;
    totalTxs: number;
    uniqueWallets: number;
    dailyBreakdown: DailyMetric[];
    weekOverWeekGrowth: number;
  }
  
  async function monitorProgramActivity(
    programAddress: string
  ): Promise<ProgramActivityReport | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(
      'solana-mainnet',
      programAddress,
      { pageSize: 100 }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', resp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const txs = resp.data?.items ?? [];
    const totalTxs = resp.data?.pagination?.total_count ?? txs.length;
  
    const dailyMap = new Map<string, { txCount: number; wallets: Set<string> }>();
    const allWallets = new Set<string>();
  
    for (const tx of txs) {
      const date = (tx.block_signed_at ?? '').slice(0, 10);
      if (!date) continue;
      if (!dailyMap.has(date)) dailyMap.set(date, { txCount: 0, wallets: new Set() });
      const day = dailyMap.get(date)!;
      day.txCount++;
      if (tx.from_address) {
        day.wallets.add(tx.from_address);
        allWallets.add(tx.from_address);
      }
    }
  
    const dailyBreakdown: DailyMetric[] = Array.from(dailyMap.entries())
      .sort(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b))
      .map(([date, { txCount, wallets }]) => ({ date, txCount, uniqueWallets: wallets.size }));
  
    const n = dailyBreakdown.length;
    const thisWeekTxs = dailyBreakdown.slice(-7).reduce((s, d) => s + d.txCount, 0);
    const lastWeekTxs = dailyBreakdown.slice(Math.max(0, n - 14), n - 7).reduce((s, d) => s + d.txCount, 0);
    const weekOverWeekGrowth =
      lastWeekTxs > 0 ? parseFloat((((thisWeekTxs - lastWeekTxs) / lastWeekTxs) * 100).toFixed(2)) : 0;
  
    return { programAddress, totalTxs, uniqueWallets: allWallets.size, dailyBreakdown, weekOverWeekGrowth };
  }
  
  // Usage
  monitorProgramActivity('JUP6LkbZbjS1jKKwapdHNy74zcZ3tLUZoi5QNyVTaV4').then((report) => {
    if (report) console.log(`WoW growth: ${report.weekOverWeekGrowth}% | ${report.uniqueWallets} unique wallets`);
  });
  ```

**solana-smart-money-tracker**
  Description: Identify high-alpha Solana wallets using GoldRush: fetch recent token buys via transaction history, calculate realized PnL from decoded swap history, and surface wallets with consistently profitable trades (>2x average return over 30 days). Once you have a watchlist, subscribe to their real-time activity stream to receive instant push notifications the moment they buy a new token — before it shows up on Dexscreener. Build a Solana copy-trading radar, alpha group feed, or smart-money signal layer for your trading agent.
  Use case: Build a Solana copy-trading radar that identifies consistently profitable wallets and sends real-time alerts the moment they buy a new token.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/
  Cost: 2 credits/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (The Solana wallet address to analyze for smart money signals), page_size (Number of transactions to analyze (use 100 for sufficient history)), page (Page number to paginate through 30-day transaction history)
  Response fields: items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].successful, items[].from_address, items[].fees_paid, items[].value, items[].log_events[].sender_name, items[].log_events[].decoded.name, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-smart-money-tracker
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface SmartMoneyProfile {
    walletAddress: string;
    totalSwaps: number;
    successfulSwaps: number;
    winRate: number;
    mostUsedDex: string;
    recentBuys: Array<{ token: string; blockTime: string; txHash: string }>;
    isSmartMoney: boolean;
  }
  
  const DEX_NAMES = ['jupiter', 'raydium', 'orca', 'meteora'];
  const MIN_WIN_RATE = 60;
  const MIN_SWAP_COUNT = 10;
  
  async function analyzeSmartMoney(walletAddress: string): Promise<SmartMoneyProfile | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(
      'solana-mainnet',
      walletAddress,
      { pageSize: 100 }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', resp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const txs = resp.data?.items ?? [];
    const swapTxs = txs.filter((tx) =>
      tx.log_events?.some((e) => DEX_NAMES.some((d) => e.sender_name?.toLowerCase().includes(d)))
    );
  
    const successfulSwaps = swapTxs.filter((tx) => tx.successful).length;
    const winRate = swapTxs.length > 0 ? (successfulSwaps / swapTxs.length) * 100 : 0;
  
    const dexCounts = new Map<string, number>();
    for (const tx of swapTxs) {
      const dexName = tx.log_events?.find((e) =>
        DEX_NAMES.some((d) => e.sender_name?.toLowerCase().includes(d))
      )?.sender_name ?? 'Unknown';
      dexCounts.set(dexName, (dexCounts.get(dexName) ?? 0) + 1);
    }
    const mostUsedDex = [...dexCounts.entries()].sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])[0]?.[0] ?? 'Unknown';
  
    const recentBuys = swapTxs.slice(0, 5).map((tx) => ({
      token: tx.log_events?.[0]?.decoded?.params?.[0]?.value ?? 'unknown',
      blockTime: tx.block_signed_at ?? '',
      txHash: tx.tx_hash ?? '',
    }));
  
    return {
      walletAddress,
      totalSwaps: swapTxs.length,
      successfulSwaps,
      winRate: parseFloat(winRate.toFixed(2)),
      mostUsedDex,
      recentBuys,
      isSmartMoney: winRate >= MIN_WIN_RATE && swapTxs.length >= MIN_SWAP_COUNT,
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  analyzeSmartMoney('7Np41oeYqPefeAfUZx9j5yCLrMQZMDyApbJAKhgQFMRm').then((profile) => {
    if (profile) console.log(`Smart money: ${profile.isSmartMoney} | Win rate: ${profile.winRate}%`);
  });
  ```

**token-ohlcv-chart**
  Description: Real-time OHLCV candlestick data for any token — stream live candles via WebSocket for TradingView-style charts.
  Use case: Power a real-time TradingView-style candlestick chart for any token — subscribe to live 1m/5m/1h OHLCV candles via WebSocket and update the chart as new candles close.
  Endpoints: wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql — ohlcvCandlesForToken subscription
  Cost: Streaming subscription
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, arbitrum, optimism, avalanche
  Key params: tokenAddress (ERC-20 token contract address), chainName (EVM chain name (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), resolution (Candle resolution: 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 1d)
  Response fields: timestamp, open, high, low, close, volume
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-ohlcv-chart
  ```typescript
  import { createClient } from 'graphql-ws';
  
  const wsClient = createClient({
    url: 'wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql',
    connectionParams: { apiKey: process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY },
  });
  
  async function streamOhlcvCandles(
    tokenAddress: string,
    chainName: string,
    resolution: '1m' | '5m' | '15m' | '1h' | '4h' | '1d',
    onCandle: (candle: any) => void
  ) {
    const sub = wsClient.iterate({
      query: `subscription OHLCV($token: String!, $chain: String!, $res: String!) {
        ohlcvCandlesForToken(tokenAddress: $token, chainName: $chain, resolution: $res) {
          timestamp open high low close volume
        }
      }`,
      variables: { token: tokenAddress, chain: chainName, res: resolution },
    });
  
    for await (const event of sub) {
      const candle = event.data?.ohlcvCandlesForToken;
      if (candle) onCandle(candle);
    }
  }
  
  // Usage: stream 5-minute candles
  streamOhlcvCandles(
    '0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000006', // WETH on Base
    'base-mainnet',
    '5m',
    candle => console.log(`Close: $${candle.close}, Volume: ${candle.volume}`)
  );
  ```

**token-price-history**
  Description: Fetch historical spot prices and OHLCV data for any ERC-20 token onchain.
  Use case: Power a price history chart for any ERC-20 token — show daily USD close prices over a custom date range for portfolio analytics and DeFi dashboards.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/pricing/historical_by_addresses_v2/{chainName}/{quoteCurrency}/{contractAddresses}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), quoteCurrency (Quote currency symbol (e.g. USD)), contractAddress (ERC-20 token contract address)
  Response fields: items[].contract_address, items[].prices[].date, items[].prices[].price
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-price-history
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getTokenPriceHistory(
    chainName: string,
    contractAddress: string
  ) {
    const resp = await client.PricingService.getTokenPrices(
      chainName,
      'USD',
      contractAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const token = resp.data.items[0];
    return {
      address: token.contract_address,
      prices: (token.prices ?? []).map(p => ({
        date: p.date,
        priceUsd: p.price,
      })),
    };
  }
  ```

**wallet-activity-heatmap**
  Description: Generate a GitHub-style activity heatmap showing daily transaction counts for any wallet — visualize on-chain behaviour patterns.
  Use case: Render a year-long activity heatmap for a wallet address showing which days had the most transactions — like a GitHub contributions chart but for on-chain activity.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_summary/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_v3/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address), pageNumber (Page number for paginating through historical transactions)
  Response fields: items[].total_count, items[].earliest_transaction.block_signed_at, items[].latest_transaction.block_signed_at, items[].block_signed_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/wallet-activity-heatmap
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function buildActivityHeatmap(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const summaryResp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionSummary(
      chainName,
      walletAddress
    );
  
    if (summaryResp.error) throw new Error(summaryResp.error_message);
  
    // Fetch recent page of txs and bucket by day
    const txResp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(
      chainName,
      walletAddress,
      0
    );
  
    if (txResp.error) throw new Error(txResp.error_message);
  
    const heatmap: Record<string, number> = {};
    for (const tx of txResp.data.items) {
      const day = String(tx.block_signed_at).slice(0, 10);
      heatmap[day] = (heatmap[day] ?? 0) + 1;
    }
  
    return {
      summary: summaryResp.data.items[0],
      heatmap,
    };
  }
  ```

**wallet-comparison-tool**
  Description: Compare two wallet addresses side-by-side — token holdings, transaction count, wallet age, and NFT inventory across the same chain.
  Use case: Build a wallet vs wallet comparison page that shows both addresses' portfolio value, transaction history depth, wallet age, top assets, and NFTs side by side.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_summary/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_nft/
  Cost: ~4 credits total
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain to compare wallets on), walletA (First wallet address to compare), walletB (Second wallet address to compare)
  Response fields: walletA.netWorth, walletA.txCount, walletA.nftCount, walletA.topAssets[], walletA.walletAge, walletB.netWorth, walletB.txCount, walletB.nftCount, walletB.topAssets[], walletB.walletAge
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/wallet-comparison-tool
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function buildWalletProfile(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const [balResp, summaryResp, nftResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionSummary(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.NftService.getNftsForAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
    ]);
  
    const tokens = (balResp.data?.items ?? []).filter(t => !t.is_spam && (t.quote ?? 0) > 0);
    const netWorth = tokens.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0);
    const summary = summaryResp.data?.items?.[0];
    const nfts = nftResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const nftCount = nfts.reduce((s, col) => s + (col.nft_data?.length ?? 0), 0);
    const firstTx = summary?.earliest_transaction?.block_signed_at;
    const walletAgeDays = firstTx
      ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(firstTx).getTime()) / 86_400_000)
      : null;
  
    return {
      address: walletAddress,
      netWorth,
      txCount: summary?.total_count ?? 0,
      nftCount,
      walletAgeDays,
      firstTxDate: firstTx,
      topAssets: tokens.slice(0, 5).map(t => ({
        symbol: t.contract_ticker_symbol,
        quote: t.quote,
      })),
    };
  }
  
  async function compareWallets(chainName: string, walletA: string, walletB: string) {
    const [profileA, profileB] = await Promise.all([
      buildWalletProfile(chainName, walletA),
      buildWalletProfile(chainName, walletB),
    ]);
    return { walletA: profileA, walletB: profileB };
  }
  ```

**whale-wallet-watchlist**
  Description: Monitor a list of whale wallets in real-time — subscribe to live transaction streams for multiple addresses and alert when they move large amounts.
  Use case: Build a whale alert dashboard that monitors 10-50 known smart money wallets simultaneously and fires notifications the moment any of them execute a transaction above a threshold.
  Endpoints: wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql — walletTxs subscription, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/
  Cost: Streaming subscription per wallet
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, arbitrum, optimism, avalanche
  Key params: walletAddresses (Array of whale wallet addresses to monitor simultaneously), chainName (EVM chain to monitor — e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet), minValueUsd (Minimum USD transaction value to trigger an alert)
  Response fields: txHash, blockSignedAt, value, fromAddress, toAddress, transfers[].tokenSymbol, transfers[].delta
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/whale-wallet-watchlist
  ```typescript
  import { createClient } from 'graphql-ws';
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const wsClient = createClient({
    url: 'wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql',
    connectionParams: { apiKey: process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY },
  });
  const apiClient = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  function watchWhaleWallets(
    walletAddresses: string[],
    chainName: string,
    minValueUsd: number,
    onAlert: (alert: any) => void
  ) {
    const subscriptions = walletAddresses.map(walletAddress => {
      return wsClient.subscribe(
        {
          query: `subscription WalletTxs($addr: String!, $chain: String!) {
            walletTxs(walletAddress: $addr, chainName: $chain) {
              txHash blockSignedAt value fromAddress toAddress
              transfers { tokenSymbol delta fromAddress toAddress }
            }
          }`,
          variables: { addr: walletAddress, chain: chainName },
        },
        {
          next: ({ data }: any) => {
            const tx = data?.walletTxs;
            if (!tx) return;
            const nativeValue = parseFloat(tx.value ?? '0') / 1e18;
            const hasLargeTransfer = (tx.transfers ?? []).some(
              (t: any) => Math.abs(parseFloat(t.delta ?? '0')) > minValueUsd
            );
            if (nativeValue > 0 || hasLargeTransfer) {
              onAlert({ wallet: walletAddress, chainName, ...tx });
            }
          },
          error: (err) => console.error(`Stream error for ${walletAddress}:`, err),
          complete: () => {},
        }
      );
    });
  
    return () => subscriptions.forEach(unsub => unsub());
  }
  ```

### AI Agents (13 skills)

**agent-counterparty-verification**
  Description: Verify counterparty wallets before agent-initiated transactions using risk signals.
  Use case: Build a pre-transaction risk check for AI agents — before sending tokens, verify the recipient is not a flagged address, has a real transaction history, and has no dangerous approvals.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/approvals/{walletAddress}/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_summary/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x address of the counterparty wallet to verify)
  Response fields: items[].token_address, items[].token_ticker_symbol, items[].spender_address, items[].spender_name, items[].value_at_risk, items[].risk_factor, items[].total_count, items[].earliest_transaction.block_signed_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/agent-counterparty-verification
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function verifyCounterparty(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const [approvalsResp, summaryResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.SecurityService.getApprovals(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionSummary(chainName, walletAddress),
    ]);
  
    if (approvalsResp.error) throw new Error(approvalsResp.error_message);
    if (summaryResp.error) throw new Error(summaryResp.error_message);
  
    const highRisk = approvalsResp.data.items.filter(
      a => (a.risk_factor ?? 0) > 0.7
    );
  
    const summary = summaryResp.data.items[0];
    const firstTx = summary?.earliest_transaction?.block_signed_at;
    const ageInDays = firstTx
      ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(firstTx).getTime()) / 86_400_000)
      : 0;
  
    return {
      walletAgeInDays: ageInDays,
      totalTransactions: summary?.total_count ?? 0,
      highRiskApprovals: highRisk.length,
      riskScore: highRisk.length > 0 ? 'HIGH' : ageInDays < 7 ? 'MEDIUM' : 'LOW',
    };
  }
  ```

**agent-data-budget-manager**
  Description: Track and optimise GoldRush API credit consumption across AI agent workflows.
  Use case: Build a credit-aware AI agent wrapper that checks chain sync status before issuing queries and tracks cumulative API credit spend across a multi-step workflow.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/chains/status/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (Optional EVM chain to check sync status for (e.g. eth-mainnet) — omit for all chains), noTestnets (Filter: exclude testnet chains from results (filter client-side, default true))
  Response fields: items[].name, items[].synced_block_height, items[].synced_blocked_signed_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/agent-data-budget-manager
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  let totalCreditsSpent = 0;
  
  async function checkChainFreshness(chainName: string) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getAllChainStatus();
    totalCreditsSpent += 0.1;
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const chain = resp.data.items.find(c => c.name === chainName);
    if (!chain) throw new Error(`Chain ${chainName} not found`);
  
    const lagMs = chain.synced_blocked_signed_at
      ? Date.now() - new Date(chain.synced_blocked_signed_at).getTime()
      : Infinity;
  
    return {
      isFresh: lagMs < 60_000,      // synced within last 60s
      lagSeconds: Math.floor(lagMs / 1000),
      syncedBlock: chain.synced_block_height,
      totalCreditsSpent,
    };
  }
  ```

**agent-fleet-monitor**
  Description: Monitor a fleet of AI agent wallets for health, balances, and activity in one view.
  Use case: Build a fleet health dashboard for autonomous agents — monitor gas balances, flag wallets that need refuelling, and surface any unusual on-chain activity across a set of agent addresses.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_summary/
  Cost: 1 credit/wallet
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain the agents operate on), walletAddresses (Array of agent wallet addresses to monitor)
  Response fields: items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].total_count, items[].latest_transaction.block_signed_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/agent-fleet-monitor
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function monitorAgentFleet(chainName: string, agentWallets: string[]) {
    const results = await Promise.all(
      agentWallets.map(async (addr) => {
        const [balResp, sumResp] = await Promise.all([
          client.BalanceService.getNativeTokenBalance(chainName, addr),
          client.TransactionService.getTransactionSummary(chainName, addr),
        ]);
  
        const nativeBalance = balResp.error ? 0 : Number(balResp.data.items[0]?.balance) / 1e18;
        const summary = sumResp.error ? null : sumResp.data.items[0];
  
        return {
          address: addr,
          nativeBalance,
          needsRefuel: nativeBalance < 0.01,
          totalTxs: summary?.total_count ?? 0,
          lastActive: summary?.latest_transaction?.block_signed_at ?? null,
        };
      })
    );
    return results;
  }
  ```

**agent-wallet-skill**
  Description: Give AI agents the ability to read wallet state, balances, and transaction data.
  Use case: Equip a Claude or GPT agent with the ability to answer questions like 'What tokens does this wallet hold?' and 'What did this wallet do recently?' using live on-chain data.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_v3/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address the agent is querying)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].successful
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/agent-wallet-skill
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  // Composable wallet skill for AI agents
  export const walletSkill = {
    name: 'get_wallet_data',
    description: 'Fetch token balances and recent transactions for a wallet address',
    async execute(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
      const [balResp, txResp] = await Promise.all([
        client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
        client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(chainName, walletAddress, 0),
      ]);
  
      if (balResp.error) throw new Error(balResp.error_message);
      if (txResp.error) throw new Error(txResp.error_message);
  
      const totalUsd = balResp.data.items.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0);
      return {
        totalUsd,
        topTokens: balResp.data.items.slice(0, 5).map(t => t.contract_ticker_symbol),
        recentTxCount: txResp.data.items.length,
      };
    },
  };
  ```

**claude-goldrush-skill**
  Description: Claude agent skill for querying GoldRush APIs — fetch wallet data, prices, and NFTs.
  Use case: Enable a Claude agent to answer on-chain questions like 'What is the ETH balance of vitalik.eth?' or 'Show me the last 5 transactions for this address' using real GoldRush data.
  Endpoints: Multiple GoldRush endpoints wrapped as Claude tools
  Cost: Varies by tool called
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: GOLDRUSH_API_KEY (GoldRush API key set as environment variable), chainName (EVM chain to query (passed by the agent at runtime)), walletAddress (Target wallet address (extracted from user prompt by Claude))
  Response fields: items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/claude-goldrush-skill
  ```typescript
  import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk';
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const anthropic = new Anthropic();
  const gr = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  const tools: Anthropic.Tool[] = [{
    name: 'get_wallet_balances',
    description: 'Fetch all token balances for a wallet on a given EVM chain',
    input_schema: {
      type: 'object',
      properties: {
        chainName: { type: 'string' },
        walletAddress: { type: 'string' },
      },
      required: ['chainName', 'walletAddress'],
    },
  }];
  
  async function runWalletAgent(userQuery: string) {
    const msg = await anthropic.messages.create({
      model: 'claude-opus-4-5',
      max_tokens: 1024,
      tools,
      messages: [{ role: 'user', content: userQuery }],
    });
    return msg;
  }
  ```

**goldrush-mcp-server**
  Description: Model Context Protocol server exposing GoldRush APIs to LLMs and AI agents.
  Use case: Connect Claude Desktop or Cursor to GoldRush so the AI can answer on-chain questions directly — no custom code needed, just configure the MCP server and start asking questions.
  Endpoints: MCP tool: get_wallet_balances, MCP tool: get_transaction_history, MCP tool: get_token_price
  Cost: Varies by tool called
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: GOLDRUSH_API_KEY (GoldRush API key configured in MCP server environment), chainName (EVM chain to query), walletAddress (Target wallet address)
  Response fields: items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].tx_hash
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/goldrush-mcp-server
  ```typescript
  // Install: npx @covalenthq/goldrush-mcp-server
  // Or add to Claude Desktop config:
  // ~/.config/claude/claude_desktop_config.json
  
  // Example MCP config:
  const mcpConfig = {
    mcpServers: {
      goldrush: {
        command: 'npx',
        args: ['-y', '@covalenthq/goldrush-mcp-server'],
        env: {
          GOLDRUSH_API_KEY: 'your_api_key_here',
        },
      },
    },
  };
  
  // Once configured, Claude Desktop can answer:
  // 'What tokens does 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045 hold on Ethereum?'
  // 'Show me the last 10 transactions for vitalik.eth'
  // 'What is the current ETH price?'
  ```

**realtime-wallet-stream**
  Description: Subscribe to live wallet activity events via WebSocket — zero polling, instant updates.
  Use case: Build a live transaction alert system for an AI agent — trigger actions (e.g. notify, rebalance, log) the instant any on-chain activity occurs for a monitored wallet.
  Endpoints: wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql — walletTxs subscription
  Cost: Streaming subscription
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +8 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to monitor), chainName (EVM chain name (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet))
  Response fields: txHash, blockSignedAt, value, fromAddress, toAddress, transfers[].tokenSymbol, transfers[].delta
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/realtime-wallet-stream
  ```typescript
  import { createClient } from 'graphql-ws';
  
  const wsClient = createClient({
    url: 'wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql',
    connectionParams: { apiKey: process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY },
  });
  
  async function watchWallet(
    walletAddress: string,
    chainName: string,
    onEvent: (tx: any) => void
  ) {
    const subscription = wsClient.iterate({
      query: `subscription Watch($addr: String!, $chain: String!) {
        walletTxs(walletAddress: $addr, chainName: $chain) {
          txHash blockSignedAt value fromAddress toAddress
          transfers { tokenSymbol delta }
        }
      }`,
      variables: { addr: walletAddress, chain: chainName },
    });
  
    for await (const event of subscription) {
      if (event.data?.walletTxs) {
        onEvent(event.data.walletTxs);
      }
    }
  }
  
  // Usage: trigger agent action on every new transaction
  watchWallet('0xYourWallet', 'eth-mainnet', tx => {
    console.log('New tx detected:', tx.txHash);
  });
  ```

**solana-agent-defi-navigator**
  Description: Pull real-time Jupiter-aggregated route data and Raydium/Orca pool metrics via GoldRush to give an AI agent the swap intelligence it needs: best output amount, price impact, pool liquidity depth, and fee tier — all in one structured call. Feed this into an agent's decision loop to autonomously execute optimal swaps on Solana — if price impact exceeds threshold, hold; otherwise sign and submit.
  Use case: Build an autonomous Solana DeFi agent that evaluates swap routes, checks price impact and pool liquidity, and executes trades only when conditions meet a configurable risk threshold.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/, GET /v1/pricing/tickers/v2/?chain-names=solana-mainnet
  Cost: 2 credits/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (Agent wallet address to check balances and transaction history), inputMint (SPL token mint address being sold in the swap), outputMint (SPL token mint address being bought in the swap), maxPriceImpactPct (Maximum acceptable price impact before blocking the swap), page_size (Number of recent transactions to analyze for context)
  Response fields: items[].contract_address, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].quote_rate, items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].log_events[].sender_name, items[].log_events[].decoded.name
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-agent-defi-navigator
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface SwapDecision {
    canSwap: boolean;
    reason: string;
    inputTokenBalance: number;
    inputTokenSymbol: string;
    estimatedOutputUsd: number;
    recentSwapCount: number;
  }
  
  async function evaluateSwap(
    agentWallet: string,
    inputMint: string,
    maxPriceImpactPct = 2
  ): Promise<SwapDecision> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const [balResp, txResp] = await Promise.all([
  ```
