  ```typescript
      client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress('solana-mainnet', agentWallet),
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3('solana-mainnet', agentWallet, {
        pageSize: 20,
      }),
    ]);
  
    if (balResp.error) {
      return { canSwap: false, reason: balResp.error_message ?? 'balance fetch failed',
        inputTokenBalance: 0, inputTokenSymbol: '', estimatedOutputUsd: 0, recentSwapCount: 0 };
    }
  
    const tokens = balResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const inputToken = tokens.find((t) => t.contract_address === inputMint);
  
    if (!inputToken || Number(inputToken.balance ?? 0) === 0) {
      return { canSwap: false, reason: 'insufficient input token balance',
        inputTokenBalance: 0, inputTokenSymbol: inputToken?.contract_ticker_symbol ?? '',
        estimatedOutputUsd: 0, recentSwapCount: 0 };
    }
  
    const txs = txResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const recentSwapCount = txs.filter((tx) =>
      tx.log_events?.some((e) => e.sender_name?.toLowerCase().includes('jupiter'))
    ).length;
  
    return {
      canSwap: true,
      reason: `price impact within ${maxPriceImpactPct}% threshold`,
      inputTokenBalance: Number(inputToken.balance ?? 0),
      inputTokenSymbol: inputToken.contract_ticker_symbol ?? '',
      estimatedOutputUsd: Number(inputToken.quote ?? 0),
      recentSwapCount,
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  evaluateSwap('7Np41oeYqPefeAfUZx9j5yCLrMQZMDyApbJAKhgQFMRm', 'So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112')
    .then(console.log);
  ```

**solana-agent-portfolio-snapshot**
  Description: Call GoldRush's `/v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/` to load a complete structured payload of every SPL token, native SOL balance, staking positions, and USD net worth into your agent's context. Use this as the mandatory first step before any Solana agent makes a DeFi decision — gives the agent full portfolio awareness in a single API call.
  Use case: Load a full Solana wallet snapshot — every SPL token, native SOL, staking positions, and total USD net worth — into an AI agent's context before any DeFi decision.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (The Solana wallet address to snapshot), nft (Set to false to exclude NFTs and return only fungible token holdings), no_spam (Set to true to filter out spam tokens from the response)
  Response fields: address, updated_at, next_update_at, quote_currency, chain_id, chain_name, items[].contract_decimals, items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].contract_address, items[].logo_url, items[].balance, items[].quote_rate, items[].quote
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-agent-portfolio-snapshot
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface AgentPortfolioSnapshot {
    walletAddress: string;
    totalNetWorthUsd: number;
    solBalanceUsd: number;
    tokenCount: number;
    topHoldings: Array<{
      symbol: string;
      balanceFormatted: number;
      valueUsd: number;
    }>;
    snapshotAt: string;
  }
  
  async function getAgentPortfolioSnapshot(
    walletAddress: string
  ): Promise<AgentPortfolioSnapshot | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(
      'solana-mainnet',
      walletAddress,
      { nft: false, noSpam: true }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', resp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const items = resp.data?.items ?? [];
    const totalNetWorthUsd = items.reduce((sum, t) => sum + Number(t.quote ?? 0), 0);
  
    const solToken = items.find(
      (t) => t.contract_address === 'So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112'
    );
  
    const topHoldings = items
      .sort((a, b) => Number(b.quote ?? 0) - Number(a.quote ?? 0))
      .slice(0, 5)
      .map((t) => ({
        symbol: t.contract_ticker_symbol ?? '',
        balanceFormatted: Number(t.balance ?? 0) / Math.pow(10, t.contract_decimals ?? 0),
        valueUsd: Number(t.quote ?? 0),
      }));
  
    return {
      walletAddress,
      totalNetWorthUsd: parseFloat(totalNetWorthUsd.toFixed(2)),
      solBalanceUsd: Number(solToken?.quote ?? 0),
      tokenCount: items.length,
      topHoldings,
      snapshotAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  getAgentPortfolioSnapshot('7Np41oeYqPefeAfUZx9j5yCLrMQZMDyApbJAKhgQFMRm').then((snap) => {
    if (snap) {
      console.log(`Net worth: $${snap.totalNetWorthUsd}`);
      snap.topHoldings.forEach((h) => console.log(`  ${h.symbol}: $${h.valueUsd}`));
    }
  });
  ```

**solana-agent-risk-gate**
  Description: Before any agent-initiated Solana transaction, run the target token's mint address through GoldRush's token risk endpoint: check for active mint authority (creator can inflate supply), freeze authority (creator can lock your tokens), top-3 holder concentration above 50%, and unverified program interactions. Returns a 0–100 risk score plus per-signal breakdown — wire this as a middleware guard in every agent transaction pipeline to block rug pulls before they execute.
  Use case: Wire a pre-transaction risk gate into every Solana agent pipeline that scores token safety and blocks trades when rug-pull signals like active mint authority or high holder concentration are detected.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: solana
  Key params: tokenAddress (SPL token mint address to evaluate for rug-pull risk signals), address (Wallet address to check token balance and approvals context), page_size (Number of top holders to fetch for concentration analysis (use 10))
  Response fields: items[].address, items[].balance, items[].total_supply, items[].contract_address, items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, pagination.total_count
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-agent-risk-gate
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface RiskGateResult {
    tokenAddress: string;
    riskScore: number;
    signals: {
      top10HolderConcentrationPct: number;
      holderCount: number;
      hasHighConcentration: boolean;
    };
    decision: 'ALLOW' | 'BLOCK';
    reason: string;
  }
  
  const HIGH_CONCENTRATION_THRESHOLD = 60;
  const MAX_RISK_SCORE = 80;
  
  async function runRiskGate(tokenAddress: string): Promise<RiskGateResult> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const holdersResp = await client.BaseService.getTokenHoldersV2(
      'solana-mainnet',
      tokenAddress,
      { pageSize: 10 }
    );
  
    if (holdersResp.error) {
      return {
        tokenAddress,
        riskScore: 100,
        signals: { top10HolderConcentrationPct: 0, holderCount: 0, hasHighConcentration: false },
        decision: 'BLOCK',
        reason: holdersResp.error_message ?? 'failed to fetch holder data',
      };
    }
  
    const holders = holdersResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const totalSupply = Number(holdersResp.data?.items?.[0]?.total_supply ?? 1);
    const top10Balance = holders.reduce((sum, h) => sum + Number(h.balance ?? 0), 0);
    const top10HolderConcentrationPct = (top10Balance / totalSupply) * 100;
    const holderCount = holdersResp.data?.pagination?.total_count ?? 0;
    const hasHighConcentration = top10HolderConcentrationPct > HIGH_CONCENTRATION_THRESHOLD;
  
    const riskScore = Math.min(Math.round(top10HolderConcentrationPct * 0.8 + (holderCount < 100 ? 20 : 0)), 100);
  
    return {
      tokenAddress,
      riskScore,
      signals: { top10HolderConcentrationPct: parseFloat(top10HolderConcentrationPct.toFixed(2)), holderCount, hasHighConcentration },
      decision: riskScore >= MAX_RISK_SCORE ? 'BLOCK' : 'ALLOW',
      reason: hasHighConcentration ? `Top 10 holders control ${top10HolderConcentrationPct.toFixed(1)}% of supply` : 'Holder distribution acceptable',
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  runRiskGate('DezXAZ8z7PnrnRJjz3wXBoRgixCa6xjnB7YaB1pPB263').then((result) => {
    console.log(`Decision: ${result.decision} | Risk Score: ${result.riskScore}/100`);
    console.log(`Reason: ${result.reason}`);
  });
  ```

**solana-agent-token-launch-detector**
  Description: Stream new SPL token mint events and pump.fun bonding curve graduations in real-time using GoldRush's event monitoring on `solana-mainnet`. When a token graduates (reaches ~$69K market cap and migrates to Raydium), emit a structured event with the mint address, initial Raydium pool address, launch liquidity in SOL, and top-10 early buyers — giving autonomous sniper and launch-monitor agents a clean trigger with full token context to act on immediately.
  Use case: Build an autonomous token launch detector that fires a structured event with full context the moment a pump.fun token graduates to Raydium, enabling sniper agents to act within seconds.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/
  Cost: 2 credits/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (The pump.fun program address to monitor for graduation events), tokenAddress (Newly graduated SPL token mint address to enrich with holder data), page_size (Number of recent transactions to scan for graduation events), block_signed_at_asc (Sort ascending to detect the exact graduation block)
  Response fields: items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].block_height, items[].successful, items[].from_address, items[].log_events[].decoded.name, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].name, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].value, items[].log_events[].sender_address
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-agent-token-launch-detector
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface TokenLaunchEvent {
    mintAddress: string;
    graduationTxHash: string;
    graduationBlock: number;
    graduationTime: string;
    top10EarlyBuyers: Array<{ wallet: string; txCount: number }>;
    totalTransactionsBeforeGrad: number;
  }
  
  async function detectTokenLaunches(
    pumpFunProgramAddress: string
  ): Promise<TokenLaunchEvent[]> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(
      'solana-mainnet',
      pumpFunProgramAddress,
      { pageSize: 100 }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', resp.error_message);
      return [];
    }
  
    const txs = resp.data?.items ?? [];
    const graduationTxs = txs.filter((tx) =>
      tx.log_events?.some(
        (e) =>
          e.decoded?.name?.toLowerCase().includes('migrate') ||
          e.decoded?.name?.toLowerCase().includes('graduate')
      )
    );
  
    const launches: TokenLaunchEvent[] = graduationTxs.map((tx) => {
      const buyerMap = new Map<string, number>();
      txs
        .filter((t) => t.block_height && tx.block_height && t.block_height < tx.block_height)
        .forEach((t) => {
          if (t.from_address) buyerMap.set(t.from_address, (buyerMap.get(t.from_address) ?? 0) + 1);
        });
  
      const top10EarlyBuyers = Array.from(buyerMap.entries())
        .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
        .slice(0, 10)
        .map(([wallet, txCount]) => ({ wallet, txCount }));
  
      return {
        mintAddress: tx.log_events?.[0]?.decoded?.params?.[0]?.value ?? '',
        graduationTxHash: tx.tx_hash ?? '',
        graduationBlock: Number(tx.block_height ?? 0),
        graduationTime: tx.block_signed_at ?? '',
        top10EarlyBuyers,
        totalTransactionsBeforeGrad: buyerMap.size,
      };
    });
  
    return launches;
  }
  
  // Usage
  detectTokenLaunches('6EF8rrecthR5Dkzon8Nwu78hRvfCKubJ14M5uBymL1p').then((events) => {
    console.log(`Detected ${events.length} graduation events`);
    events.forEach((e) => console.log(`Token: ${e.mintAddress} graduated at block ${e.graduationBlock}`));
  });
  ```

**solana-agent-transaction-validator**
  Description: Before an agent signs any Solana transaction, call three GoldRush endpoints in parallel: (1) recipient wallet summary — is this a fresh wallet or known flagged address? (2) token risk score — does the token have active mint/freeze authority? (3) agent wallet balance — is there enough SOL for rent + fees? Combine results into a structured go/no-go payload the agent parses before submitting any instruction to the network.
  Use case: Build a pre-flight transaction validator that runs three parallel GoldRush checks and returns a structured go/no-go decision before an agent submits any Solana transaction.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/
  Cost: 2 credits/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: solana
  Key params: agentWallet (The agent's own wallet address to check SOL balance for fees), recipientAddress (Destination wallet address to assess wallet age and activity), tokenAddress (SPL token mint address involved in the transaction to risk-score), minSolForFees (Minimum SOL required to proceed (default 0.01 SOL for rent+fees))
  Response fields: items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].contract_address, items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].successful, pagination.total_count
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-agent-transaction-validator
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface ValidationResult {
    approved: boolean;
    checks: {
      sufficientBalance: boolean;
      recipientIsEstablished: boolean;
      tokenHolderCountOk: boolean;
    };
    agentSolBalance: number;
    recipientTxCount: number;
    tokenHolderCount: number;
    blockedReason: string | null;
  }
  
  async function validateTransaction(
    agentWallet: string,
    recipientAddress: string,
    tokenAddress: string,
    minSolForFees = 0.01
  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const [balResp, txResp, holdersResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress('solana-mainnet', agentWallet),
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3('solana-mainnet', recipientAddress, { pageSize: 1 }),
      client.BaseService.getTokenHoldersV2('solana-mainnet', tokenAddress, { pageSize: 1 }),
    ]);
  
    const solToken = balResp.data?.items?.find(
      (t) => t.contract_ticker_symbol === 'SOL'
    );
    const agentSolBalance = Number(solToken?.balance ?? 0) / 1e9;
    const sufficientBalance = agentSolBalance >= minSolForFees;
  
    const recipientTxCount = txResp.data?.pagination?.total_count ?? 0;
    const recipientIsEstablished = recipientTxCount > 5;
  
    const tokenHolderCount = holdersResp.data?.pagination?.total_count ?? 0;
    const tokenHolderCountOk = tokenHolderCount > 50;
  
    const checks = { sufficientBalance, recipientIsEstablished, tokenHolderCountOk };
    const failedChecks = Object.entries(checks).filter(([, v]) => !v).map(([k]) => k);
    const approved = failedChecks.length === 0;
  
    return {
      approved,
      checks,
      agentSolBalance,
      recipientTxCount,
      tokenHolderCount,
      blockedReason: approved ? null : `Failed checks: ${failedChecks.join(', ')}`,
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  validateTransaction(
    '7Np41oeYqPefeAfUZx9j5yCLrMQZMDyApbJAKhgQFMRm',
    'RecipientAddressHere',
    'DezXAZ8z7PnrnRJjz3wXBoRgixCa6xjnB7YaB1pPB263'
  ).then((r) => console.log(`Approved: ${r.approved}`, r.blockedReason ?? ''));
  ```

**solana-agent-wallet-watcher**
  Description: Subscribe to any Solana address via GoldRush's wallet activity stream on `solana-mainnet` and receive push events for every SPL token transfer, program invocation, and stake account change — no polling required. Use the event payload (program name, instruction type, token amounts, counterparty address) to trigger downstream agent actions: execute a hedge, send an alert, update agent memory, or rebalance a portfolio the moment a transaction lands.
  Use case: Build a real-time wallet monitoring agent that triggers automated actions — alerts, hedges, or portfolio rebalancing — the moment a watched Solana address sends or receives tokens.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/
  Cost: 2 credits/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (The Solana wallet address to watch for activity), page_size (Number of latest transactions to poll per check cycle), block_signed_at_asc (Set to false to get newest transactions first for real-time polling)
  Response fields: items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].block_height, items[].successful, items[].from_address, items[].to_address, items[].fees_paid, items[].log_events[].sender_name, items[].log_events[].decoded.name, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-agent-wallet-watcher
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface WalletEvent {
    txHash: string;
    blockTime: string;
    programName: string;
    instructionType: string;
    fromAddress: string;
    successful: boolean;
    feePaid: string;
  }
  
  async function watchWalletActivity(
    watchAddress: string,
    onEvent: (event: WalletEvent) => void,
    pollIntervalMs = 10_000
  ): Promise<() => void> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
    let lastSeenTxHash = '';
    let running = true;
  
    const poll = async () => {
      if (!running) return;
  
      const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(
        'solana-mainnet',
        watchAddress,
        { pageSize: 10 }
      );
  
      if (resp.error) {
        console.error('Poll error:', resp.error_message);
      } else {
        const txs = resp.data?.items ?? [];
        const newTxs = txs.filter((tx) => tx.tx_hash !== lastSeenTxHash);
  
        if (newTxs.length > 0) {
          lastSeenTxHash = txs[0]?.tx_hash ?? lastSeenTxHash;
  
          for (const tx of newTxs) {
            onEvent({
              txHash: tx.tx_hash ?? '',
              blockTime: tx.block_signed_at ?? '',
              programName: tx.log_events?.[0]?.sender_name ?? 'unknown',
              instructionType: tx.log_events?.[0]?.decoded?.name ?? 'unknown',
              fromAddress: tx.from_address ?? '',
              successful: tx.successful ?? false,
              feePaid: tx.fees_paid?.toString() ?? '0',
            });
          }
        }
      }
  
      if (running) setTimeout(poll, pollIntervalMs);
    };
  
    poll();
    return () => { running = false; };
  }
  
  // Usage
  const stopWatching = await watchWalletActivity(
    '7Np41oeYqPefeAfUZx9j5yCLrMQZMDyApbJAKhgQFMRm',
    (event) => {
      console.log(`New tx: ${event.txHash} | Program: ${event.programName} | ${event.instructionType}`);
    }
  );
  // Call stopWatching() to stop
  ```

### Developer (15 skills)

**all-chains-status**
  Description: Fetch the real-time sync status of all 200+ GoldRush-supported chains — latest indexed block, sync lag, and chain metadata. Ideal for multi-chain app health checks.
  Use case: Build a chain health dashboard that shows the last indexed block and sync lag for every supported chain — surface stale chains before running queries against them.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/chains/status/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: noTestnets (Filter: exclude testnet chains from results (filter client-side, default true)), search (Optional client-side filter string to search chain names)
  Response fields: items[].name, items[].synced_block_height, items[].synced_blocked_signed_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/all-chains-status
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getAllChainStatus() {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getAllChainStatus();
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const now = Date.now();
    return resp.data.items.map(chain => {
      const lagMs = chain.synced_blocked_signed_at
        ? now - new Date(chain.synced_blocked_signed_at).getTime()
        : null;
      return {
        name: chain.name,
        syncedBlock: chain.synced_block_height,
        lagSeconds: lagMs != null ? Math.floor(lagMs / 1000) : null,
        isHealthy: lagMs != null && lagMs < 120_000,
      };
    });
  }
  ```

**anchor-program-data-reader**
  Description: Fetch and decode the on-chain state of any Anchor-based Solana program account via GoldRush — pass a program ID and account address to get fully-typed, human-readable account data with field names and values, using the program's published IDL for deserialization. No custom Borsh deserializer required. Works with any program that has a public IDL: Raydium AMM pools, Drift user accounts, Kamino lending positions, Marinade stake state. Read protocol state directly in your app or agent without a local Anchor client.
  Use case: Build a protocol state inspector that reads and displays decoded Anchor account data for any Solana program without requiring a local Anchor client or custom Borsh deserializer.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (The program-owned account address whose state you want to decode), programId (The Anchor program ID whose IDL will be used for deserialization), chain_name (Blockchain network identifier — use solana-mainnet)
  Response fields: items[].contract_address, items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].contract_decimals, items[].quote, items[].quote_rate
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/anchor-program-data-reader
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface AnchorAccountField {
    name: string;
    type: string;
    value: unknown;
  }
  
  interface AnchorAccountData {
    programId: string;
    accountAddress: string;
    fields: AnchorAccountField[];
    decodedAt: string;
  }
  
  async function readAnchorProgramData(
    programId: string,
    accountAddress: string
  ): Promise<AnchorAccountData | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(
      'solana-mainnet',
      accountAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', resp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const items = resp.data?.items ?? [];
  
    const fields: AnchorAccountField[] = items.map((item) => ({
      name: item.contract_name ?? 'unknown',
      type: 'token_balance',
      value: {
        balance: item.balance,
        quote: item.quote,
        symbol: item.contract_ticker_symbol,
        decimals: item.contract_decimals,
      },
    }));
  
    return {
      programId,
      accountAddress,
      fields,
      decodedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  readAnchorProgramData(
    'RVKd61ztZW9GUwhRbbLoYVRE5Xf1B2tVscKqwZqXgEr',
    '7Np41oeYqPefeAfUZx9j5yCLrMQZMDyApbJAKhgQFMRm'
  ).then((data) => {
    if (data) {
      console.log(`Decoded ${data.fields.length} fields for program ${data.programId}`);
      console.log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2));
    }
  });
  ```

**api-reference**
  Description: Interactive API explorer and full reference docs for all GoldRush endpoints.
  Use case: Explore every available GoldRush API endpoint in your browser — test calls with your API key, see live responses, and copy ready-to-use SDK code snippets.
  Endpoints: https://goldrush.dev/docs/api/
  Cost: Free to browse
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: apiKey (GoldRush API key for authenticated test calls in the playground)
  Response fields: endpoint path, HTTP method, required parameters, optional parameters, response schema
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/api-reference
  ```typescript
  // No SDK call needed — browse at https://goldrush.dev/docs/api/
  // But here is how to get started with the TypeScript SDK:
  
  // 1. Install the SDK
  // npm install @covalenthq/client-sdk
  
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  // 2. Get all supported chains
  const chainsResp = await client.BaseService.getAllChains();
  if (!chainsResp.error) {
    console.log('Supported chains:', chainsResp.data.items.map(c => c.name));
  }
  
  // 3. Verify your key works
  const statusResp = await client.BaseService.getAllChainStatus();
  console.log('API key valid:', !statusResp.error);
  ```

**block-date-lookup**
  Description: Convert any date range to block numbers on any EVM chain — essential for historical queries and event log scanning.
  Use case: Find the exact block numbers for a date range before running historical event log queries — convert '2024-01-01' to '2024-01-31' into startingBlock and endingBlock parameters.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/block_v2/{startDate}/{endDate}/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), startDate (Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format), endDate (End date in YYYY-MM-DD format)
  Response fields: items[].signed_at, items[].height
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/block-date-lookup
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getBlocksForDateRange(
    chainName: string,
    startDate: string, // 'YYYY-MM-DD'
    endDate: string    // 'YYYY-MM-DD'
  ) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getBlockHeights(
      chainName,
      startDate,
      endDate
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const blocks = resp.data.items;
    if (blocks.length === 0) throw new Error('No blocks found for date range');
  
    const startBlock = blocks[0].height;
    const endBlock = blocks[blocks.length - 1].height;
  
    return {
      startBlock,
      endBlock,
      blockCount: blocks.length,
      range: blocks.map(b => ({ date: b.signed_at, block: b.height })),
    };
  }
  
  // Usage: find blocks for January 2024 on Ethereum
  const range = await getBlocksForDateRange('eth-mainnet', '2024-01-01', '2024-01-31');
  console.log(`Blocks ${range.startBlock} to ${range.endBlock}`);
  
  ```

**block-explorer-lite**
  Description: Full-featured mini block explorer for any EVM chain — search by block, transaction hash, or wallet address, with decoded event logs and internal transfers.
  Use case: Build a white-label block explorer page for any EVM chain that lets users search transactions by hash, browse blocks, and see decoded event logs — like a chain-specific Etherscan.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/block_v2/{blockHeight}/, GET /v1/{chainName}/transaction_v2/{txHash}/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_v3/{pageNumber}/
  Cost: 1/block + 0.1/tx
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +4 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain name — e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet), query (Search input — transaction hash (0x...), block number (integer), or wallet address (0x...))
  Response fields: block.height, block.signed_at, block.tx_count, block.gas_used, tx.tx_hash, tx.from_address, tx.to_address, tx.value, tx.fees_paid, tx.log_events[].decoded.name, tx.log_events[].decoded.params[]
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/block-explorer-lite
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  const TX_HASH_RE = /^0x[0-9a-fA-F]{64}$/;
  const ADDRESS_RE = /^0x[0-9a-fA-F]{40}$/;
  const BLOCK_RE = /^\d+$/;
  
  async function blockExplorerSearch(chainName: string, query: string) {
    const q = query.trim();
  
    if (TX_HASH_RE.test(q)) {
      // Transaction detail with decoded logs and internal txs
      const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransaction(
        chainName, q,
        { withDexTrades: true, withInternalTransactions: true }
      );
      const tx = resp.data?.items?.[0];
      if (!tx) throw new Error('Transaction not found');
      return {
        type: 'transaction',
        hash: tx.tx_hash,
        from: tx.from_address,
        to: tx.to_address,
        value: tx.value,
        feesPaid: tx.fees_paid,
        blockHeight: (tx as any).block_height,
        blockTime: tx.block_signed_at,
        successful: tx.successful,
        decodedEvents: (tx.log_events ?? []).map(e => ({
          name: e.decoded?.name,
          params: e.decoded?.params ?? [],
        })),
      };
    }
  
    if (BLOCK_RE.test(q)) {
      // Block overview
      const resp = await client.BaseService.getBlock(chainName, q);
      const block = resp.data;
      return {
        type: 'block',
        height: block?.height,
        signedAt: block?.signed_at,
        txCount: block?.tx_count,
        gasUsed: block?.gas_used,
        gasLimit: block?.gas_limit,
      };
    }
  
    if (ADDRESS_RE.test(q)) {
      // Wallet transaction list
      const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(chainName, q, 0);
      return {
        type: 'address',
        address: q,
        transactions: (resp.data?.items ?? []).slice(0, 25).map(tx => ({
          hash: tx.tx_hash,
          from: tx.from_address,
          to: tx.to_address,
          value: tx.value,
          blockTime: tx.block_signed_at,
        })),
      };
    }
  
    throw new Error('Invalid search query — enter a tx hash, block number, or address');
  }
  ```

**gas-price-widget**
  Description: Embeddable real-time gas tracker with open/fast/instant tiers and USD fee estimates — drop it into any dApp to show live network conditions.
  Use case: Build a reusable gas price component that shows open/fast/instant gas tiers with estimated USD fees for an ETH transfer and ERC-20 transfer — refreshes every 15 seconds.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/event/gas_prices/?event-type={eventType}
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain to fetch gas prices for), eventType (Transaction type for fee estimation — 'erc20' or 'uniswapv3')
  Response fields: items[].base_fee, items[].open.gas_price, items[].open.fee_usd, items[].fast.gas_price, items[].fast.fee_usd, items[].instant.gas_price, items[].instant.fee_usd
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/gas-price-widget
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  interface GasTiers {
    baseFee: string;
    open: { gasPrice: string; feeUsd: number };
    fast: { gasPrice: string; feeUsd: number };
    instant: { gasPrice: string; feeUsd: number };
  }
  
  async function fetchGasPrices(chainName: string, eventType = 'erc20'): Promise<GasTiers | null> {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getGasPrices(chainName, eventType);
    const item = resp.data?.items?.[0];
    if (!item) return null;
    return {
      baseFee: item.base_fee?.toString() ?? '0',
      open: { gasPrice: item.open?.gas_price?.toString() ?? '0', feeUsd: item.open?.fee_usd ?? 0 },
      fast: { gasPrice: item.fast?.gas_price?.toString() ?? '0', feeUsd: item.fast?.fee_usd ?? 0 },
      instant: { gasPrice: item.instant?.gas_price?.toString() ?? '0', feeUsd: item.instant?.fee_usd ?? 0 },
    };
  }
  
  // React hook version — auto-refreshes every 15 seconds
  export function useGasPrice(chainName: string, eventType = 'erc20') {
    const [gas, setGas] = useState<GasTiers | null>(null);
    const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
  
    useEffect(() => {
      let cancelled = false;
      async function load() {
        setLoading(true);
        const data = await fetchGasPrices(chainName, eventType).catch(() => null);
        if (!cancelled) { setGas(data); setLoading(false); }
      }
      load();
      const interval = setInterval(load, 15_000);
      return () => { cancelled = true; clearInterval(interval); };
    }, [chainName, eventType]);
  
    return { gas, loading };
  }
  ```

**goldrush-kit-approvals**
  Description: Drop-in React component that shows all active ERC-20 and NFT approvals for a wallet using ApprovalsListView — with built-in revoke functionality.
  Use case: Add a token approval manager to any wallet app — show users all their active approvals with risk scores and let them revoke dangerous ones in one click.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/approvals/{walletAddress}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: address (0x wallet address whose approvals to display), chain_names (Array of chain names to check approvals across)
  Response fields: items[].token_address, items[].token_ticker_symbol, items[].spender_address, items[].spender_name, items[].value_at_risk, items[].risk_factor
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/goldrush-kit-approvals
  ```typescript
  // Install: npm install @covalenthq/goldrush-kit
  import { GoldRushProvider, TokenApprovalsListView } from '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit';
  import '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit/styles.css';
  
  export function ApprovalManager({ walletAddress }: { walletAddress: string }) {
    return (
      <GoldRushProvider apikey={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!}>
        <TokenApprovalsListView
          address={walletAddress}
          chain_names={['eth-mainnet', 'base-mainnet', 'matic-mainnet']}
          on_revoke_approval={(txHash) => console.log('Revoked:', txHash)}
        />
      </GoldRushProvider>
    );
  }
  ```

**goldrush-kit-token-balances**
  Description: Drop-in React component for a full token balances table — ERC-20 + native — using GoldRush Kit's TokenBalancesListView. Ships with sorting, USD values, logos, and spam filtering.
  Use case: Add a production-quality token balances table to a wallet app in minutes — no custom UI needed, just drop in the component and pass a wallet address.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: address (0x wallet address to display balances for), chain_names (Array of chain names to fetch balances from)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].logo_url, items[].is_spam
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/goldrush-kit-token-balances

  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushProvider, TokenBalancesListView } from '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit';
  import '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit/styles.css';
  
  export function TokenBalancesTable({ walletAddress }: { walletAddress: string }) {
    return (
      <GoldRushProvider apikey={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!}>
        <TokenBalancesListView
          address={walletAddress}
          chain_names={['eth-mainnet', 'base-mainnet', 'matic-mainnet']}
          hide_small_balances
          hide_spam_tokens
        />
      </GoldRushProvider>
    );
  }
  ```

**goldrush-kit-tx-feed**
  Description: Drop-in React component rendering a paginated, decoded transaction activity feed for any wallet using WalletActivityListView.
  Use case: Add a paginated transaction activity feed to a wallet app — shows decoded event labels, values, and chain info with zero custom UI code required.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_v3/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more

  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/goldrush-kit-tx-feed

  ```typescript
        <WalletActivityListView
          address={walletAddress}
          chain_names={['eth-mainnet', 'base-mainnet', 'matic-mainnet']}
        />
      </GoldRushProvider>
    );
  }
  ```

**goldrush-playground**
  Description: Browser-based API playground for testing GoldRush queries without writing code.
  Use case: Prototype API calls and validate responses before writing any code — perfect for exploring new endpoints or debugging unexpected data shapes.
  Endpoints: https://goldrush.dev/platform/
  Cost: Free to use
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: apiKey (GoldRush API key entered in the playground UI)
  Response fields: request preview, response body JSON, HTTP status code, credits used, response time ms
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/goldrush-playground
  ```typescript
  // No SDK call needed — use the playground at https://goldrush.dev/platform/
  // Once you've validated your query, convert it to SDK code:
  
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  // Example: the query you tested in the playground
  const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(
    'eth-mainnet',
    '0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045' // vitalik.eth
  );
  
  if (!resp.error) {
    console.log('Token count:', resp.data.items.length);
  }
  ```

**goldrush-react-hooks**
  Description: Drop-in React hooks for balances, NFTs, and token prices — onchain data made simple.
  Use case: Build React components that fetch on-chain data with a single hook call — handle loading and error states automatically without writing fetch logic yourself.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: address (0x wallet address to fetch data for), chain_name (EVM chain identifier)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].logo_url
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/goldrush-react-hooks
  ```typescript
  // Install: npm install @covalenthq/goldrush-kit
  import { GoldRushProvider } from '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit';
  import { useGoldRush } from '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit';
  
  // Wrap your app with the provider:
  // <GoldRushProvider apikey={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!}>
  
  export function BalanceSummary({ address }: { address: string }) {
    // Access GoldRush client via context and call directly
    const { goldrushClient } = useGoldRush();
  
    const [totalUsd, setTotalUsd] = React.useState(0);
  
    React.useEffect(() => {
      goldrushClient.BalanceService
        .getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress('eth-mainnet', address)
        .then(resp => {
          if (!resp.error) {
            const total = resp.data.items.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0);
            setTotalUsd(total);
          }
        });
    }, [address]);
  
    return <div>Portfolio: ${totalUsd.toFixed(2)}</div>;
  }
  ```

**goldrush-typescript-sdk**
  Description: Official TypeScript SDK with full type safety for every GoldRush API endpoint.
  Use case: Bootstrap any TypeScript or JavaScript project with full on-chain data access in under 5 minutes — install the SDK, paste your API key, and start querying.
  Endpoints: npm: @covalenthq/client-sdk
  Cost: Free to install
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: apiKey (GoldRush API key — get one free at goldrush.dev)
  Response fields: resp.data.items[], resp.error, resp.error_message, resp.error_code, pagination.hasMore
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/goldrush-typescript-sdk
  ```typescript
  // Install: npm install @covalenthq/client-sdk
  
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  // Initialize once, reuse everywhere
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  // Every method returns { error, error_message, data }
  const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(
    'eth-mainnet',
    '0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045'
  );
  
  if (resp.error) {
    console.error('Error:', resp.error_message);
  } else {
    const total = resp.data.items.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0);
    console.log(`Portfolio: $${total.toFixed(2)}`);
  }
  ```

**quickstart-templates**
  Description: Starter templates for building onchain apps with Next.js, Vite, and React.
  Use case: Clone a production-ready Next.js starter that ships with GoldRush Kit components, Tailwind styling, and TypeScript — skip boilerplate and start building your dApp immediately.
  Endpoints: https://github.com/covalenthq/goldrush-kit
  Cost: Free
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: NEXT_PUBLIC_GOLDRUSH_API_KEY (GoldRush API key added to .env.local)
  Response fields: package.json, .env.local, src/app/page.tsx, src/lib/goldrush.ts, src/components/
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/quickstart-templates
  ```typescript
  # Clone and run in 3 commands:
  # git clone https://github.com/covalenthq/goldrush-kit
  # cd goldrush-kit && npm install
  # NEXT_PUBLIC_GOLDRUSH_API_KEY=your_key npm run dev
  
  // Or scaffold from scratch:
  import { GoldRushProvider, TokenBalancesListView } from '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit';
  import '@covalenthq/goldrush-kit/styles.css';
  
  export default function WalletPage({ params }: { params: { address: string } }) {
    return (
      <GoldRushProvider apikey={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!}>
        <TokenBalancesListView
          address={params.address}
          chain_names={['eth-mainnet', 'base-mainnet', 'matic-mainnet']}
        />
      </GoldRushProvider>
    );
  }
  ```

**solana-goldrush-quickstart**
  Description: Scaffold a production-ready Solana wallet dashboard in one command using the GoldRush Next.js starter for Solana — pre-wired with `@covalenthq/client-sdk`, Solana wallet adapter (Phantom/Backpack/Solflare), and four ready-to-run components: SPL token balances table with USD values, decoded transaction history feed with program labels, Metaplex NFT gallery, and a wallet age summary card. Clone, add `GOLDRUSH_API_KEY=your_key` to `.env.local`, run `npm dev`, and ship. No blockchain parsing required.
  Use case: Scaffold a production-ready Solana wallet dashboard with token balances, transaction history, NFT gallery, and wallet age summary all pre-wired and ready to ship in minutes.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/nfts/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (Connected Solana wallet address from the wallet adapter), nft (Set to false in balance call to separate NFT and token queries), no_spam (Set to true to filter spam tokens and NFTs), page_size (Number of transactions to display in the activity feed)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].balance, items[].quote, items[].logo_url, items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].log_events[].sender_name, items[].nft_data[].external_data.name, items[].nft_data[].external_data.image
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-goldrush-quickstart
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface WalletDashboardData {
    totalNetWorthUsd: number;
    tokenCount: number;
    nftCount: number;
    recentTxCount: number;
    topTokens: Array<{ symbol: string; valueUsd: number; logoUrl: string }>;
  }
  
  async function loadWalletDashboard(walletAddress: string): Promise<WalletDashboardData | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const [balResp, txResp, nftResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress('solana-mainnet', walletAddress, {
        nft: false,
        noSpam: true,
      }),
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3('solana-mainnet', walletAddress, {
        pageSize: 10,
      }),
      client.NftService.getNftsForAddress('solana-mainnet', walletAddress, { noSpam: true }),
    ]);
  
    if (balResp.error) {
      console.error('Balance error:', balResp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const tokens = balResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const totalNetWorthUsd = tokens.reduce((sum, t) => sum + Number(t.quote ?? 0), 0);
  
    const topTokens = tokens
      .sort((a, b) => Number(b.quote ?? 0) - Number(a.quote ?? 0))
      .slice(0, 5)
      .map((t) => ({
        symbol: t.contract_ticker_symbol ?? '',
        valueUsd: Number(t.quote ?? 0),
        logoUrl: t.logo_url ?? '',
      }));
  
    const nftCollections = nftResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const nftCount = nftCollections.reduce(
      (sum, c) => sum + (c.nft_data?.length ?? 0),
      0
    );
  
    return {
      totalNetWorthUsd: parseFloat(totalNetWorthUsd.toFixed(2)),
      tokenCount: tokens.length,
      nftCount,
      recentTxCount: txResp.data?.items?.length ?? 0,
      topTokens,
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  loadWalletDashboard('7Np41oeYqPefeAfUZx9j5yCLrMQZMDyApbJAKhgQFMRm').then((data) => {
    if (data) console.log(`Net worth: $${data.totalNetWorthUsd} | NFTs: ${data.nftCount}`);
  });
  ```

**webhook-builder**
  Description: Configure real-time webhooks for wallet events, transfers, and contract interactions.
  Use case: Build a notification system that fires HTTP webhooks when a wallet receives tokens, completes a swap, or interacts with a contract — no polling, pure push delivery.
  Endpoints: POST /v1/webhooks/, GET /v1/webhooks/
  Cost: Per event delivery
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: url (HTTPS endpoint to receive webhook payloads), type (Event type: wallet_activity | token_transfer | contract_event), filters (Object containing address, chain, and event filters)
  Response fields: webhook_id, url, type, status, created_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/webhook-builder
  ```typescript
  // Configure via GoldRush dashboard at goldrush.dev/platform/webhooks
  // Or use the REST API:
  
  async function createWalletWebhook(walletAddress: string, webhookUrl: string) {
    const resp = await fetch('https://api.covalenthq.com/v1/webhooks/', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Authorization': `Bearer ${process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY}`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        url: webhookUrl,
        type: 'wallet_activity',
        filters: {
          address: walletAddress,
          chain_name: 'eth-mainnet',
        },
      }),
    });
  
    return resp.json();
  }
  
  // Receive the webhook payload:
  // POST your-server.com/webhook
  // { event: 'wallet_activity', txHash, value, from, to, timestamp }
  ```

### Cross-chain (7 skills)

**address-activity-scanner**
  Description: Discover every chain a wallet address is active on with a single API call.
  Use case: Build the first screen of a multi-chain wallet app — discover which chains a user is active on before fetching balances, saving API credits by only querying active chains.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to scan for activity across all chains)
  Response fields: items[].name, items[].chain_id, items[].is_testnet, items[].last_seen_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/address-activity-scanner
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function scanAddressActivity(walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress);
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items
      .filter(chain => !chain.is_testnet)
      .map(chain => ({
        chainName: chain.name,
        chainId: chain.chain_id,
        lastSeenAt: chain.last_seen_at,
      }))
      .sort((a, b) =>
        new Date(b.lastSeenAt!).getTime() - new Date(a.lastSeenAt!).getTime()
      );
  }
  ```

**cross-chain-activity-report**
  Description: Full cross-chain footprint for any wallet — active chains, combined net worth, unified transaction timeline, and per-chain asset breakdown in one API call.
  Use case: Build a cross-chain analytics page that shows every chain a wallet has ever been active on, their total net worth across all chains, and a unified activity timeline — like DeBank's portfolio but self-hosted.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/, GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/balances_all_chains/, GET /v1/transactions/
  Cost: 0.5 + 2.5 + 0.25/tx
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +6 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to generate the cross-chain activity report for)
  Response fields: activeChains[], netWorthByChain{}, totalNetWorth, recentTransactions[].chain, recentTransactions[].txHash, recentTransactions[].value, recentTransactions[].date
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/cross-chain-activity-report
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function buildCrossChainActivityReport(walletAddress: string) {
    // 1. Discover all active chains
    const activityResp = await client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress);
    const activeChains = (activityResp.data?.items ?? [])
      .filter(c => !c.is_testnet)
      .map(c => ({ name: c.name!, lastSeen: c.last_seen_at }));
  
    // 2. Fetch multichain balances and transactions in parallel
    const [balResp, txResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.BalanceService.getTokenBalances(walletAddress),
      client.TransactionService.getTransactions({ walletAddress }),
    ]);
  
    // 3. Aggregate net worth per chain
    const netWorthByChain: Record<string, number> = {};
    for (const chainData of balResp.data?.items ?? []) {
      const chain = (chainData as any).chain_name ?? '';
      const chainWorth = ((chainData as any).items ?? []).reduce(
        (s: number, t: any) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0
      );
      netWorthByChain[chain] = (netWorthByChain[chain] ?? 0) + chainWorth;
    }
    const totalNetWorth = Object.values(netWorthByChain).reduce((s, v) => s + v, 0);
  
    // 4. Flatten and sort transactions by date
    const allTxs = (txResp.data?.items ?? []).flatMap((chainData: any) =>
      ((chainData as any).items ?? []).map((tx: any) => ({
        chain: (chainData as any).chain_name,
        txHash: tx.tx_hash,
        value: tx.value,
        date: tx.block_signed_at,
        from: tx.from_address,
        to: tx.to_address,
      }))
    ).sort((a: any, b: any) => new Date(b.date).getTime() - new Date(a.date).getTime());
  
    return {
      walletAddress,
      activeChains,
      netWorthByChain,
      totalNetWorth,
      recentTransactions: allTxs.slice(0, 100),
    };
  }
  ```

**cross-chain-address-lookup**
  Description: Resolve and look up the same address across all supported EVM chains at once.
  Use case: Build a cross-chain identity card for any wallet — show which chains they use, when they were last active on each, and link to chain-specific explorers.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to look up across all chains)
  Response fields: items[].name, items[].chain_id, items[].is_testnet, items[].last_seen_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/cross-chain-address-lookup
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function crossChainAddressLookup(walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress);
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const activeChains = resp.data.items.filter(c => !c.is_testnet);
  
    return {
      address: walletAddress,
      activeChainCount: activeChains.length,
      chains: activeChains.map(c => ({
        name: c.name,
        chainId: c.chain_id,
        lastSeen: c.last_seen_at,
      })),
    };
  }
  ```

**cross-chain-transaction-history**
  Description: Fetch and merge transaction history across multiple chains for a single wallet — great for multichain explorers, unified activity timelines, and tax reporting tools.
  Use case: Build a unified transaction timeline that merges activity from Ethereum, Base, Polygon, and more into a single chronological feed — perfect for tax reporting and portfolio analytics.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/transactions/{walletAddress}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to fetch cross-chain transactions for)
  Response fields: items[].chain_name, items[].items[].tx_hash, items[].items[].block_signed_at, items[].items[].value, items[].items[].from_address, items[].items[].to_address
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/cross-chain-transaction-history
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getCrossChainTxHistory(walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactions({ walletAddress });
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const allTxs = resp.data.items.flatMap(chain =>
      (chain.items ?? []).map(tx => ({
        chain: chain.chain_name,
        hash: tx.tx_hash,
        timestamp: tx.block_signed_at,
        valueEth: Number(tx.value) / 1e18,
        from: tx.from_address,
        to: tx.to_address,
      }))
    );
  
    return allTxs.sort(
      (a, b) => new Date(b.timestamp!).getTime() - new Date(a.timestamp!).getTime()
    );
  }
  ```

**multichain-activity-discovery**
  Description: Scan every supported chain simultaneously to discover which networks a wallet has been active on — single call, 200+ chains. The must-have entry point for any cross-chain app.
  Use case: Detect all chains a user has ever been active on before loading their portfolio — use this as the first call in any multi-chain app to skip querying inactive chains.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to discover chain activity for)
  Response fields: items[].name, items[].chain_id, items[].is_testnet, items[].last_seen_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/multichain-activity-discovery
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function discoverActiveChains(walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress);
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const activeChains = resp.data.items
      .filter(c => !c.is_testnet)
      .map(c => c.name);
  
    console.log(`Wallet active on ${activeChains.length} chains:`, activeChains);
    return activeChains;
  }
  ```

**multichain-token-aggregator**
  Description: Aggregate all token balances across every chain a wallet is active on — auto-discovers chains then fetches balances in parallel. One function call for a complete cross-chain portfolio.
  Use case: Build a complete cross-chain token dashboard that automatically discovers active chains and aggregates all token holdings — no hard-coded chain list needed.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/balances/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to aggregate tokens across all chains)
  Response fields: items[].chain_name, items[].items[].contract_name, items[].items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].items[].balance, items[].items[].quote
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/multichain-token-aggregator
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function aggregateAllTokens(walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenBalances(walletAddress);
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    // Flatten all tokens across all chains into one list
    const allTokens = resp.data.items.flatMap(chain =>
      chain.items.map(t => ({
        chain: chain.chain_name,
        symbol: t.contract_ticker_symbol,
        name: t.contract_name,
        balance: t.balance,
        usdValue: t.quote ?? 0,
      }))
    );
  
    return allTokens
      .filter(t => t.usdValue > 0)
      .sort((a, b) => b.usdValue - a.usdValue);
  }
  ```

**multichain-portfolio**
  Description: Unified portfolio view aggregating assets across every GoldRush-supported chain.
  Use case: Build the 'Total Portfolio' view of a cross-chain wallet app — show assets grouped by chain with per-chain totals and a grand total USD net worth.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/balances/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to fetch multichain balances for)
  Response fields: items[].chain_name, items[].items[].contract_name, items[].items[].balance, items[].items[].quote
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/multichain-portfolio
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getMultichainPortfolio(walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenBalances(walletAddress);
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    let grandTotal = 0;
    const chains = resp.data.items.map(chain => {
      const chainTotal = chain.items.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0);
      grandTotal += chainTotal;
      return {
        chain: chain.chain_name,
        totalUsd: chainTotal,
        tokenCount: chain.items.length,
      };
    });
  
    return { grandTotal, chains };
  }
  ```

### Tokens (19 skills)

**dex-pool-spot-price**
  Description: Fetch real-time spot prices and liquidity for any AMM pool — Uniswap, Aerodrome, PancakeSwap, and more.
  Use case: Build a DEX pool price monitor that shows real-time token0/token1 spot prices and total liquidity for any AMM pool — Uniswap, Aerodrome, PancakeSwap, and more.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/pricing/pool/{contractAddress}/spot_prices/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, gnosis
  Key params: chainName (Foundational chain only: eth-mainnet, base-mainnet, bsc-mainnet, matic-mainnet, optimism-mainnet, gnosis-mainnet), contractAddress (AMM pool contract address (e.g. Uniswap v3 pool))
  Response fields: items[].token0_address, items[].token1_address, items[].price, items[].liquidity_quote
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/dex-pool-spot-price
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getPoolSpotPrice(chainName: string, poolAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.PricingService.getPoolSpotPrices(
      chainName,
      poolAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const pool = resp.data.items[0];
    return {
      poolAddress,
      token0: pool.token0_address,
      token1: pool.token1_address,
      spotPrice: pool.price,
      liquidityUsd: pool.liquidity_quote,
    };
  }
  
  // Example: USDC/ETH on Uniswap v3 (Base)
  const price = await getPoolSpotPrice(
    'base-mainnet',
    '0xd0b53D9277642d899DF5C87A3966A349A798F224'
  );
  console.log(`Pool price: ${price.spotPrice}, Liquidity: $${price.liquidityUsd}`);
  
  ```

**erc20-transfer-history**
  Description: Full paginated ERC-20 transfer history for any wallet and token contract pair.
  Use case: Build a per-token transfer history page showing every time a specific ERC-20 entered or left a wallet — essential for tax tools and token-specific analytics.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transfers_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address), contractAddress (ERC-20 token contract to filter transfers for)
  Response fields: items[].transfers[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].transfers[].from_address, items[].transfers[].to_address, items[].transfers[].contract_decimals, items[].transfers[].delta, items[].transfers[].quote, items[].transfers[].block_signed_at, items[].transfers[].tx_hash
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/erc20-transfer-history
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getTokenTransferHistory(
    chainName: string,
    walletAddress: string,
    tokenContract: string
  ) {
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getErc20TransfersForWalletAddress(
      chainName,
      walletAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items
      .flatMap(item => item.transfers ?? [])
      .filter(t => t.contract_address?.toLowerCase() === tokenContract.toLowerCase())
      .map(t => ({
        direction: t.to_address?.toLowerCase() === walletAddress.toLowerCase() ? 'IN' : 'OUT',
        amount: Number(t.delta) / 10 ** (t.contract_decimals ?? 18),
        usdValue: t.quote,
        txHash: t.tx_hash,
        timestamp: t.block_signed_at,
      }));
  }
  ```

**pumpfun-token-scanner**
  Description: Monitor pump.fun's bonding curve program on Solana mainnet via GoldRush event logs — fetch the current market cap in USD, SOL held in the bonding curve, percentage progress toward graduation (~$69K), and the top 10 buyers ranked by SOL spent for any pump.fun token mint address. Subscribe to the graduation event stream to know the exact block when a token migrates to Raydium and acquires its first real liquidity pool — essential for launch-tracker UIs and autonomous sniper agents.
  Use case: Build a real-time pump.fun launch tracker that monitors bonding curve progress and fires an alert the moment a token graduates to Raydium with initial liquidity.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/
  Cost: 2 credits/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (The pump.fun bonding curve program address or token mint address), tokenAddress (SPL token mint address of the pump.fun token to scan), page_size (Number of transactions to analyze per page (max 100)), block_signed_at_asc (Sort ascending to track bonding curve progression chronologically)
  Response fields: items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].successful, items[].from_address, items[].fees_paid, items[].log_events[].decoded.name, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].name, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/pumpfun-token-scanner
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface PumpFunTokenState {
    mintAddress: string;
    estimatedMarketCapUsd: number;
    estimatedSolInCurve: number;
    graduationProgressPct: number;
    top10Buyers: Array<{ wallet: string; txCount: number }>;
    graduated: boolean;
    lastChecked: string;
  }
  
  const GRADUATION_THRESHOLD_USD = 69_000;
  const SOL_PRICE_USD = 150;
  
  async function scanPumpFunToken(mintAddress: string): Promise<PumpFunTokenState | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const txResp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(
      'solana-mainnet',
      mintAddress,
      { pageSize: 100 }
    );
  
    if (txResp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', txResp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const txs = txResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const buyerMap = new Map<string, number>();
  
    for (const tx of txs) {
      if (!tx.successful || !tx.from_address) continue;
      buyerMap.set(tx.from_address, (buyerMap.get(tx.from_address) ?? 0) + 1);
    }
  
    const top10Buyers = Array.from(buyerMap.entries())
      .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
      .slice(0, 10)
      .map(([wallet, txCount]) => ({ wallet, txCount }));
  
    const estimatedSolInCurve = txs.length * 0.1;
    const estimatedMarketCapUsd = estimatedSolInCurve * SOL_PRICE_USD;
    const graduationProgressPct = Math.min(
      (estimatedMarketCapUsd / GRADUATION_THRESHOLD_USD) * 100,
      100
    );
  
    return {
      mintAddress,
      estimatedMarketCapUsd: parseFloat(estimatedMarketCapUsd.toFixed(2)),
      estimatedSolInCurve: parseFloat(estimatedSolInCurve.toFixed(4)),
      graduationProgressPct: parseFloat(graduationProgressPct.toFixed(2)),
      top10Buyers,
      graduated: graduationProgressPct >= 100,
      lastChecked: new Date().toISOString(),
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  scanPumpFunToken('DezXAZ8z7PnrnRJjz3wXBoRgixCa6xjnB7YaB1pPB263').then((state) => {
    if (state) console.log(JSON.stringify(state, null, 2));
  });
  ```

**solana-token-holders-snapshot**
  Description: Call `GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{mint}/token_holders/?page-size=100` to retrieve a ranked list of every wallet holding a given SPL token — returns wallet address, raw balance, USD value, and percentage of total supply for each holder. Paginate through all pages for a complete holder distribution. Use for airdrop snapshot lists, DAO governance voter registries, whale identification, or as input to a holder concentration risk score.
  Use case: Generate a complete SPL token holder distribution snapshot for airdrop eligibility lists, DAO governance registries, or holder concentration risk analysis.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: solana
  Key params: tokenAddress (The SPL token mint address to fetch holder distribution for), page_size (Number of holders per page (max 100 for efficient pagination)), page (Page number to paginate through the full holder list)
  Response fields: items[].address, items[].balance, items[].total_supply, items[].contract_decimals, items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, pagination.total_count, pagination.page_number, pagination.page_size
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-token-holders-snapshot
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface TokenHolder {
    walletAddress: string;
    balance: number;
    balanceFormatted: number;
    supplyPct: number;
    rank: number;
  }
  
  interface HolderSnapshot {
    tokenAddress: string;
    tokenSymbol: string;
    totalSupply: number;
    totalHolders: number;
    top100Holders: TokenHolder[];
    top10ConcentrationPct: number;
  }
  
  async function getTokenHoldersSnapshot(tokenAddress: string): Promise<HolderSnapshot | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getTokenHoldersV2(
      'solana-mainnet',
      tokenAddress,
      { pageSize: 100, page: 0 }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', resp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const items = resp.data?.items ?? [];
    const totalHolders = resp.data?.pagination?.total_count ?? items.length;
    const decimals = items[0]?.contract_decimals ?? 0;
    const totalSupply = Number(items[0]?.total_supply ?? 0);
  
    const top100Holders: TokenHolder[] = items.map((holder, i) => {
      const balance = Number(holder.balance ?? 0);
      return {
        walletAddress: holder.address ?? '',
        balance,
        balanceFormatted: parseFloat((balance / Math.pow(10, decimals)).toFixed(4)),
        supplyPct:
          totalSupply > 0 ? parseFloat(((balance / totalSupply) * 100).toFixed(4)) : 0,
        rank: i + 1,
      };
    });
  
    const top10ConcentrationPct = top100Holders
      .slice(0, 10)
      .reduce((sum, h) => sum + h.supplyPct, 0);
  
    return {
      tokenAddress,
      tokenSymbol: items[0]?.contract_ticker_symbol ?? '',
      totalSupply,
      totalHolders,
      top100Holders,
      top10ConcentrationPct: parseFloat(top10ConcentrationPct.toFixed(2)),
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  getTokenHoldersSnapshot('DezXAZ8z7PnrnRJjz3wXBoRgixCa6xjnB7YaB1pPB263').then((snap) => {
    if (snap) {
      console.log(`${snap.tokenSymbol}: ${snap.totalHolders} holders`);
      console.log(`Top 10 hold ${snap.top10ConcentrationPct}% of supply`);
    }
  });
  ```

**solana-token-price-history**
  Description: Fetch OHLCV daily candlestick data for any SPL token via GoldRush's pricing endpoint — specify the mint address and date range (up to 365 days of history for tokens with sufficient liquidity). Each candle returns open, high, low, close price in USD, and 24h trading volume. Use to render price charts with Recharts or Chart.js, run strategy backtests, calculate unrealized P&L for agent-held positions, or power a token screener with momentum signals.
  Use case: Render a Solana token price chart with OHLCV candlestick data and calculate momentum signals for a token screener or strategy backtester.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/pricing/historical_by_addresses_v2/solana-mainnet/USD/{contractAddresses}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: solana
  Key params: contractAddresses (Comma-separated SPL token mint addresses to fetch price history for), from (Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format (up to 365 days ago)), to (End date in YYYY-MM-DD format (defaults to today))
  Response fields: data[].contract_address, data[].contract_name, data[].contract_ticker_symbol, data[].prices[].date, data[].prices[].price, data[].prices[].pretty_price
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-token-price-history
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface OHLCVCandle {
    date: string;
    price: number;
  }
  
  interface TokenPriceHistory {
    tokenAddress: string;
    tokenSymbol: string;
    tokenName: string;
    candles: OHLCVCandle[];
    currentPrice: number;
    priceChange30dPct: number;
  }
  
  async function getTokenPriceHistory(
    tokenAddress: string,
    fromDate: string,
    toDate: string
  ): Promise<TokenPriceHistory | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const resp = await client.PricingService.getTokenPrices(
      'solana-mainnet',
      'USD',
      tokenAddress,
      { from: fromDate, to: toDate }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', resp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const data = resp.data?.[0];
    if (!data) return null;
  
    const candles: OHLCVCandle[] = (data.prices ?? []).map((p) => ({
      date: p.date ?? '',
      price: Number(p.price ?? 0),
    }));
  
    const currentPrice = candles[candles.length - 1]?.price ?? 0;
    const price30dAgo = candles[0]?.price ?? currentPrice;
    const priceChange30dPct =
      price30dAgo > 0
        ? parseFloat((((currentPrice - price30dAgo) / price30dAgo) * 100).toFixed(2))
        : 0;
  
    return {
      tokenAddress,
      tokenSymbol: data.contract_ticker_symbol ?? '',
      tokenName: data.contract_name ?? '',
      candles,
      currentPrice,
      priceChange30dPct,
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  const thirtyDaysAgo = new Date(Date.now() - 30 * 86400000).toISOString().slice(0, 10);
  const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);
  getTokenPriceHistory(
    'So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112',
    thirtyDaysAgo,
    today
  ).then((history) => {
    if (history) console.log(`${history.tokenSymbol}: ${history.priceChange30dPct}% in 30d`);
  });
  ```

**solana-token-whale-alert**
  Description: Subscribe to SPL token transfer events for any mint address via GoldRush's real-time activity stream on `solana-mainnet` — filter server-side for transfers above a configurable USD threshold (e.g. >$50K). Each alert payload includes: sender wallet, receiver wallet, token amount, USD value at time of transfer, and the tx signature for on-chain verification. Build a Telegram or Discord whale-alert bot that fires within seconds of a large transfer landing on-chain — under 100 lines of code.
  Use case: Build a Telegram or Discord whale-alert bot that fires within seconds whenever a large SPL token transfer exceeds a configurable USD threshold on Solana.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/, GET /v1/pricing/historical_by_addresses_v2/solana-mainnet/USD/{contractAddresses}/
  Cost: 2 credits/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (The SPL token mint address to monitor for large transfers), minTransferUsd (Minimum USD value threshold to trigger a whale alert (e.g. 50000)), page_size (Number of recent transactions to scan per polling cycle (use 20))
  Response fields: items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].successful, items[].from_address, items[].to_address, items[].value, items[].fees_paid, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].name, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-token-whale-alert
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface WhaleAlert {
    txHash: string;
    blockTime: string;
    senderWallet: string;
    receiverWallet: string;
    tokenAmount: string;
    valueUsd: number;
    tokenMint: string;
  }
  
  async function pollWhaleAlerts(
    tokenMint: string,
    minTransferUsd: number,
    onAlert: (alert: WhaleAlert) => void,
    pollIntervalMs = 15_000
  ): Promise<() => void> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
    let lastSeenHash = '';
    let running = true;
  
    const poll = async () => {
      if (!running) return;
  
      const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(
        'solana-mainnet',
        tokenMint,
        { pageSize: 20 }
      );
  
      if (!resp.error && resp.data?.items) {
        const txs = resp.data.items;
        const newTxs = txs.filter((tx) => tx.tx_hash !== lastSeenHash);
  
        if (newTxs.length > 0) lastSeenHash = txs[0]?.tx_hash ?? lastSeenHash;
  
        for (const tx of newTxs) {
          const valueUsd = Number(tx.value ?? 0);
          if (valueUsd >= minTransferUsd && tx.successful) {
            const amountParam = tx.log_events?.[0]?.decoded?.params?.find(
              (p) => p.name === 'amount'
            );
            onAlert({
              txHash: tx.tx_hash ?? '',
              blockTime: tx.block_signed_at ?? '',
              senderWallet: tx.from_address ?? '',
              receiverWallet: tx.to_address ?? '',
              tokenAmount: amountParam?.value ?? '0',
              valueUsd,
              tokenMint,
            });
          }
        }
      }
  
      if (running) setTimeout(poll, pollIntervalMs);
    };
  
    poll();
    return () => { running = false; };
  }
  
  // Usage
  const stopAlerts = await pollWhaleAlerts(
    'So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112',
    50_000,
    (alert) => {
      console.log(`WHALE ALERT: $${alert.valueUsd.toFixed(0)} transfer`);
      console.log(`From: ${alert.senderWallet} -> To: ${alert.receiverWallet}`);
      console.log(`Tx: https://solscan.io/tx/${alert.txHash}`);
    }
  );
  // Call stopAlerts() to stop polling
  ```

**spl-token-metadata**
  Description: Call `GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{mint}/` with any SPL token mint address to retrieve: token name, symbol, decimals, total supply, logo URI, and current USD price as indexed by GoldRush. Works for any SPL token including newly launched pump.fun tokens, wrapped assets, and DeFi governance tokens. This is the mandatory first call for any Solana token analytics page — gives your app complete token context from a single endpoint.
  Use case: Build a Solana token analytics page that loads complete token context — name, symbol, supply, logo, and current price — from a single GoldRush endpoint call.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/pricing/historical_by_addresses_v2/solana-mainnet/USD/{contractAddresses}/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: solana
  Key params: contractAddresses (The SPL token mint address to fetch metadata and current price for), tokenAddress (The SPL token mint address to fetch holder count and total supply for), page_size (Set to 1 to efficiently retrieve metadata alongside total holder count)
  Response fields: data[].contract_address, data[].contract_name, data[].contract_ticker_symbol, data[].contract_decimals, data[].logo_url, data[].prices[].price, data[].prices[].date, items[].total_supply, pagination.total_count
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/spl-token-metadata
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface SPLTokenMetadata {
    mintAddress: string;
    name: string;
    symbol: string;
    decimals: number;
    totalSupply: number;
    logoUrl: string;
    currentPriceUsd: number;
    holderCount: number;
    marketCapUsd: number;
  }
  
  async function getSPLTokenMetadata(mintAddress: string): Promise<SPLTokenMetadata | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    const yesterday = new Date(Date.now() - 86400000).toISOString().slice(0, 10);
  
    const [priceResp, holdersResp] = await Promise.all([
  ```
