  ```typescript
      client.PricingService.getTokenPrices('solana-mainnet', 'USD', mintAddress, { from: yesterday, to: today }),
      client.BaseService.getTokenHoldersV2('solana-mainnet', mintAddress, { pageSize: 1 }),
    ]);
  
    if (priceResp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', priceResp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const tokenData = priceResp.data?.[0];
    if (!tokenData) return null;
  
    const latestPrice = tokenData.prices?.[tokenData.prices.length - 1];
    const currentPriceUsd = Number(latestPrice?.price ?? 0);
    const holderCount = holdersResp.data?.pagination?.total_count ?? 0;
    const totalSupplyRaw = Number(holdersResp.data?.items?.[0]?.total_supply ?? 0);
    const decimals = tokenData.contract_decimals ?? 0;
    const totalSupply = totalSupplyRaw / Math.pow(10, decimals);
    const marketCapUsd = parseFloat((totalSupply * currentPriceUsd).toFixed(2));
  
    return {
      mintAddress,
      name: tokenData.contract_name ?? '',
      symbol: tokenData.contract_ticker_symbol ?? '',
      decimals,
      totalSupply,
      logoUrl: tokenData.logo_url ?? '',
      currentPriceUsd,
      holderCount,
      marketCapUsd,
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  getSPLTokenMetadata('So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112').then((meta) => {
    if (meta) {
      console.log(`${meta.symbol} (${meta.name})`);
      console.log(`Price: $${meta.currentPriceUsd} | Holders: ${meta.holderCount} | MCap: $${meta.marketCapUsd}`);
    }
  });
  ```

**spot-price-ticker**
  Description: Live spot prices for thousands of tokens indexed by GoldRush across all chains.
  Use case: Build a live token price ticker or price lookup widget — fetch the current USD price for any ERC-20 by contract address with a single API call.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/pricing/historical_by_addresses_v2/{chainName}/{quoteCurrency}/{contractAddresses}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), quoteCurrency (Quote currency (e.g. USD)), contractAddresses (Comma-separated list of ERC-20 contract addresses)
  Response fields: items[].contract_address, items[].prices[].date, items[].prices[].price
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/spot-price-ticker
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getSpotPrice(chainName: string, contractAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.PricingService.getTokenPrices(
      chainName,
      'USD',
      contractAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const token = resp.data.items[0];
    const latest = token.prices?.[0];
  
    return {
      address: token.contract_address,
      priceUsd: latest?.price ?? null,
      date: latest?.date ?? null,
    };
  }
  ```

**token-approval-scanner**
  Description: Identify and audit all ERC-20 token approvals granted by any wallet address.
  Use case: Build a token approval checker that shows every active approval a wallet has granted — highlight unlimited approvals and flag spenders by risk level.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/approvals/{walletAddress}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, matic-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address to scan approvals for)
  Response fields: items[].token_address, items[].token_ticker_symbol, items[].spender_address, items[].spender_name, items[].value_at_risk, items[].risk_factor
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-approval-scanner
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function scanTokenApprovals(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.SecurityService.getApprovals(
      chainName,
      walletAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items.map(a => ({
      token: a.token_ticker_symbol,
      tokenAddress: a.token_address,
      spender: a.spender_name ?? a.spender_address,
      valueAtRisk: a.value_at_risk,
      riskFactor: a.risk_factor,
      isHighRisk: (a.risk_factor ?? 0) > 0.7,
    }));
  }
  ```

**token-holder-list**
  Description: Retrieve a ranked list of holders for any token contract address across chains.
  Use case: Build a token holder leaderboard showing the top 100 holders of any ERC-20 — display wallet addresses, balances, and ownership percentages for community dashboards.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, gnosis
  Key params: chainName (Foundational chain only: eth-mainnet, base-mainnet, bsc-mainnet, matic-mainnet, optimism-mainnet, gnosis-mainnet), tokenAddress (ERC-20 token contract address), pageNumber (Page number (0-indexed)), pageSize (Items per page (max 1000))
  Response fields: items[].address, items[].balance, items[].total_supply, items[].balance_percent_of_total
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-holder-list
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getTokenHolders(
    chainName: string,
    tokenAddress: string,
    pageNumber = 0
  ) {
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenHoldersV2ForTokenAddress(
      chainName,
      tokenAddress,
      { pageNumber, pageSize: 100 }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items.map((h, rank) => ({
      rank: rank + 1 + pageNumber * 100,
      address: h.address,
      balance: h.balance,
      percentOfSupply: h.balance_percent_of_total,
    }));
  }
  ```

**token-holders-leaderboard**
  Description: Paginated leaderboard of every token holder with balance, percentage of supply, and rank — build airdrop lists, DAO voter registries, and holder dashboards.
  Use case: Build a token holders leaderboard page for any ERC-20 showing the top 1000 holders sorted by balance, with % of total supply and wallet labels.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/
  Cost: 0.02/holder
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, gnosis
  Key params: chainName (Foundational chain only — eth-mainnet, base-mainnet, bsc-mainnet, matic-mainnet, optimism-mainnet, gnosis-mainnet), tokenAddress (ERC-20 token contract address), pageNumber (0-indexed page number), pageSize (Holders per page — max 100)
  Response fields: items[].address, items[].balance, items[].total_supply, items[].balance_percent_of_total
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-holders-leaderboard
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  interface HolderRow {
    rank: number;
    address: string;
    balance: string;
    pctOfSupply: number;
  }
  
  async function getTokenHoldersLeaderboard(
    chainName: string,
    tokenAddress: string,
    pageNumber = 0,
    pageSize = 100
  ): Promise<{ holders: HolderRow[]; totalSupply: string; hasMore: boolean }> {
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenHoldersV2ForTokenAddress(
      chainName, tokenAddress, { pageNumber, pageSize }
    );
  
    const items = resp.data?.items ?? [];
    const totalSupply = items[0]?.total_supply ?? '0';
  
    const holders: HolderRow[] = items.map((item, idx) => ({
      rank: pageNumber * pageSize + idx + 1,
      address: item.address ?? '',
      balance: item.balance ?? '0',
      pctOfSupply: item.balance_percent_of_total ?? 0,
    }));
  
    return {
      holders,
      totalSupply,
      hasMore: items.length === pageSize,
    };
  }
  
  // Fetch top 1000 holders (10 pages)
  async function getTop1000Holders(chainName: string, tokenAddress: string) {
    const allHolders: HolderRow[] = [];
    for (let page = 0; page < 10; page++) {
      const { holders, hasMore } = await getTokenHoldersLeaderboard(chainName, tokenAddress, page);
      allHolders.push(...holders);
      if (!hasMore) break;
    }
    return allHolders;
  }
  ```

**token-holders-snapshot**
  Description: Paginated list of every holder of an ERC-20 token with balance, percentage of supply, and address. Essential for airdrops, governance snapshots, and holder analytics.
  Use case: Export a complete holder snapshot for any ERC-20 token at a specific block height — use for airdrop distribution lists, governance vote eligibility, and holder analytics.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/page
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, gnosis
  Key params: chainName (Foundational chain only: eth-mainnet, base-mainnet, bsc-mainnet, matic-mainnet, optimism-mainnet, gnosis-mainnet), tokenAddress (ERC-20 token contract address), pageNumber (Page number starting from 0), pageSize (Results per page (max 1000))
  Response fields: items[].address, items[].balance, items[].total_supply, items[].balance_percent_of_total
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-holders-snapshot
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getAllHolders(chainName: string, tokenAddress: string) {
    const allHolders: Array<{ address: string; balance: string; pct: number }> = [];
    let page = 0;
  
    while (true) {
      const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenHoldersV2ForTokenAddress(
        chainName,
        tokenAddress,
        { pageNumber: page, pageSize: 1000 }
      );
  
      if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
      const items = resp.data.items;
      if (items.length === 0) break;
  
      allHolders.push(...items.map(h => ({
        address: h.address,
        balance: h.balance,
        pct: h.balance_percent_of_total ?? 0,
      })));
  
      if (items.length < 1000) break;
      page++;
    }
  
    return allHolders;
  }
  ```

**token-metadata-api**
  Description: Fetch logos, decimals, contract info, and supply data for any ERC-20 token.
  Use case: Build a token info card that shows name, symbol, logo, decimals, and current USD price for any ERC-20 — use getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress and filter by contract address.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (Any wallet address that holds the token), contractAddress (ERC-20 contract address to get metadata for)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].contract_address, items[].contract_decimals, items[].logo_url, items[].quote_rate, items[].type
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-metadata-api
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  // Use a well-known holder to fetch token metadata
  const VITALIK = '0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045';
  
  async function getTokenMetadata(chainName: string, tokenAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(
      chainName,
      VITALIK
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const token = resp.data.items.find(
      t => t.contract_address?.toLowerCase() === tokenAddress.toLowerCase()
    );
  
    if (!token) throw new Error('Token not found in response');
  
    return {
      name: token.contract_name,
      symbol: token.contract_ticker_symbol,
      decimals: token.contract_decimals,
      logo: token.logo_url,
      priceUsd: token.quote_rate,
      type: token.type,
    };
  }
  ```

**token-price-chart**
  Description: Historical USD price for one or more token contracts on any chain. Powers price charts, backtesting, P&L calculation, and DeFi analytics.
  Use case: Render a 30-day price history chart for any ERC-20 token — show daily USD close prices as a line or candlestick chart for DeFi dashboards and portfolio analytics.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/pricing/historical_by_addresses_v2/{chainName}/{quoteCurrency}/{contractAddresses}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), quoteCurrency (Quote currency (USD)), contractAddresses (ERC-20 contract address)
  Response fields: items[].contract_address, items[].prices[].date, items[].prices[].price
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-price-chart
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getTokenPriceChart(chainName: string, contractAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.PricingService.getTokenPrices(
      chainName,
      'USD',
      contractAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const token = resp.data.items[0];
    const chartData = (token.prices ?? [])
      .map(p => ({ date: p.date, priceUsd: p.price }))
      .sort((a, b) => String(a.date).localeCompare(String(b.date)));
  
    return {
      address: token.contract_address,
      chartData,
      currentPrice: chartData[chartData.length - 1]?.priceUsd ?? null,
    };
  }
  ```

**token-search**
  Description: Search any token by name or symbol across all indexed chains — returns address, chain, and logo for instant token lookup UIs.
  Use case: Build a token search input that lets users find any token by name or ticker — returns contract address, chain, and logo for use in swap UIs and token selectors.
  Endpoints: wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql — searchToken query
  Cost: Free
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, arbitrum, optimism, avalanche, solana
  Key params: q (Search query string — token name or symbol (e.g. 'USDC', 'Uniswap'))
  Response fields: name, symbol, address, chainName, logoUrl
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-search
  ```typescript
  import { createClient } from 'graphql-ws';
  
  const wsClient = createClient({
    url: 'wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql',
    connectionParams: { apiKey: process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY },
  });
  
  async function searchToken(query: string) {
    return new Promise<any[]>((resolve, reject) => {
      wsClient.subscribe(
        {
          query: `query SearchToken($q: String!) {
            searchToken(q: $q) {
              name symbol address chainName logoUrl
            }
          }`,
          variables: { q: query },
        },
        {
          next: (data) => resolve(data.data?.searchToken ?? []),
          error: reject,
          complete: () => {},
        }
      );
    });
  }
  
  // Usage
  const results = await searchToken('USDC');
  results.forEach(t => console.log(`${t.symbol} on ${t.chainName}: ${t.address}`));
  
  ```

**token-supply-metadata**
  Description: Fetch token contract metadata including name, symbol, decimals, total supply, logo, and current USD price from a single endpoint.
  Use case: Build a token directory that shows name, symbol, decimals, logo, and live USD price for any ERC-20 token in your app — no separate metadata endpoint needed.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (Any wallet address holding the token)
  Response fields: items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, items[].contract_address, items[].contract_decimals, items[].logo_url, items[].quote_rate, items[].type
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-supply-metadata
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getTokenSupplyAndMeta(
    chainName: string,
    walletAddress: string
  ) {
    const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(
      chainName,
      walletAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items
      .filter(t => t.type === 'cryptocurrency' && !t.is_spam)
      .map(t => ({
        name: t.contract_name,
        symbol: t.contract_ticker_symbol,
        address: t.contract_address,
        decimals: t.contract_decimals,
        logo: t.logo_url,
        priceUsd: t.quote_rate,
      }));
  }
  ```

**token-transfer-stream**
  Description: Real-time stream of ERC-20 transfers for any token or wallet address.
  Use case: Build a live token transfer alert that fires instantly when any ERC-20 token enters or leaves a watched wallet — no polling, pure WebSocket delivery.
  Endpoints: wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql — walletTxs subscription
  Cost: Streaming subscription
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +8 more
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to stream transfers for), chainName (EVM chain name (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet))
  Response fields: txHash, blockSignedAt, fromAddress, toAddress, transfers[].tokenSymbol, transfers[].delta
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-transfer-stream
  ```typescript
  import { createClient } from 'graphql-ws';
  
  const wsClient = createClient({
    url: 'wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql',
    connectionParams: { apiKey: process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY },
  });
  
  async function streamTokenTransfers(
    walletAddress: string,
    chainName: string,
    onTransfer: (transfer: any) => void
  ) {
    const sub = wsClient.iterate({
      query: `subscription TokenTransfers($addr: String!, $chain: String!) {
        walletTxs(walletAddress: $addr, chainName: $chain) {
          txHash blockSignedAt fromAddress toAddress
          transfers { tokenSymbol delta }
        }
      }`,
      variables: { addr: walletAddress, chain: chainName },
    });
  
    for await (const event of sub) {
      const tx = event.data?.walletTxs;
      if (tx?.transfers?.length) {
        tx.transfers.forEach((t: any) => onTransfer({ ...t, txHash: tx.txHash }));
      }
    }
  }
  ```

**token-unlock-tracker**
  Description: Monitor Transfer events from known vesting/lock contracts to track token unlocks as they happen — build an unlock calendar or alert system.
  Use case: Build a token unlock monitor that watches a vesting contract address for Transfer events and alerts when tokens are released, showing recipient, amount, and USD value.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/events/topics/{topicHash}/, GET /v1/{chainName}/block_v2/{startDate}/{endDate}/
  Cost: 0.01/event
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain where the vesting contract is deployed), vestingContractAddress (Address of the vesting or lock contract to watch for outgoing transfers), topicHash (ERC-20 Transfer topic hash — 0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef), startDate (Start date for unlock scan in YYYY-MM-DD format), endDate (End date for unlock scan in YYYY-MM-DD format)
  Response fields: items[].block_signed_at, items[].tx_hash, items[].decoded.params[].name, items[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-unlock-tracker
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  const TRANSFER_TOPIC = '0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef';
  
  async function trackTokenUnlocks(
    chainName: string,
    vestingContractAddress: string,
    startDate: string,
    endDate: string
  ) {
    // 1. Convert date range to block heights
    const blocksResp = await client.BaseService.getBlockHeights(chainName, startDate, endDate);
    const blocks = blocksResp.data?.items ?? [];
    if (blocks.length < 2) throw new Error('Could not resolve block range for dates');
  
    const startBlock = blocks[0].height!;
    const endBlock = blocks[blocks.length - 1].height!;
  
    // 2. Fetch Transfer events from the vesting contract in the date range
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getLogEventsByTopicHash(
      chainName, TRANSFER_TOPIC,
      { startingBlock: startBlock, endingBlock: endBlock }
    );
  
    const unlockEvents = (resp.data?.items ?? []).filter(
      e => e.sender_address?.toLowerCase() === vestingContractAddress.toLowerCase()
    );
  
    return unlockEvents.map(event => {
      const params = event.decoded?.params ?? [];
      const from = params.find((p: any) => p.name === 'from')?.value ?? '';
      const to = params.find((p: any) => p.name === 'to')?.value ?? '';
      const rawValue = params.find((p: any) => p.name === 'value')?.value ?? '0';
      return {
        date: event.block_signed_at,
        txHash: event.tx_hash,
        from, to,
        rawAmount: rawValue,
      };
    });
  }
  ```

**token-whale-alert**
  Description: Detect large ERC-20 transfers for any token in real-time — subscribe to wallet activity and fire alerts when a single transfer exceeds a USD threshold.
  Use case: Build a whale alert bot that monitors a specific token contract and fires a notification whenever a transfer exceeds $100k — shows sender, receiver, amount, and USD value.
  Endpoints: wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql — walletTxs subscription
  Cost: Streaming subscription
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, arbitrum, optimism, avalanche
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain to monitor for large token transfers), tokenAddress (ERC-20 token contract address to watch for whale moves), minUsdThreshold (Minimum USD transfer value to trigger a whale alert)
  Response fields: txHash, blockSignedAt, transfers[].tokenSymbol, transfers[].delta, transfers[].fromAddress, transfers[].toAddress
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-whale-alert
  ```typescript
  import { createClient } from 'graphql-ws';
  
  const wsClient = createClient({
    url: 'wss://streaming.goldrushdata.com/graphql',
    connectionParams: { apiKey: process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY },
  });
  
  interface WhaleAlert {
    txHash: string; blockSignedAt: string;
    tokenSymbol: string; amount: string;
    usdValue: number; from: string; to: string;
  }
  
  function watchTokenWhaleAlerts(
    chainName: string,
    tokenAddress: string,
    minUsdThreshold: number,
    onAlert: (alert: WhaleAlert) => void
  ) {
    return wsClient.subscribe(
      {
        query: `subscription WhaleWatch($addr: String!, $chain: String!) {
          walletTxs(walletAddress: $addr, chainName: $chain) {
            txHash blockSignedAt
            transfers {
              tokenSymbol tokenAddress delta quoteRate
              fromAddress toAddress
            }
          }
        }`,
        variables: { addr: tokenAddress, chain: chainName },
      },
      {
        next: ({ data }: any) => {
          const tx = data?.walletTxs;
          if (!tx) return;
          for (const transfer of tx.transfers ?? []) {
            if (transfer.tokenAddress?.toLowerCase() !== tokenAddress.toLowerCase()) continue;
            const rawAmount = parseFloat(transfer.delta ?? '0');
            const usdValue = Math.abs(rawAmount) * (transfer.quoteRate ?? 0);
            if (usdValue < minUsdThreshold) continue;
            onAlert({
              txHash: tx.txHash,
              blockSignedAt: tx.blockSignedAt,
              tokenSymbol: transfer.tokenSymbol,
              amount: transfer.delta,
              usdValue,
              from: transfer.fromAddress,
              to: transfer.toAddress,
            });
          }
        },
        error: (err) => console.error('Stream error:', err),
        complete: () => {},
      }
    );
  }
  ```

### Identity (8 skills)

**airdrop-eligibility-checker**
  Description: Check whether a wallet meets configurable airdrop criteria — transaction count, wallet age, multi-chain activity, specific protocol interactions, and minimum balance thresholds.
  Use case: Build an airdrop checker tool that evaluates wallets against a configurable set of criteria and returns a pass/fail with score breakdown — like the Arbitrum or OP airdrop checker.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_summary/, GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/{chainName}/events/address/{contractAddress}/
  Cost: ~3-5 credits/check
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche
  Key params: walletAddress (Wallet address to evaluate for airdrop eligibility), criteria.minTxCount (Minimum number of transactions required), criteria.minWalletAgeDays (Minimum wallet age in days), criteria.minChainCount (Minimum number of chains the wallet must be active on), criteria.requiredContracts (Array of contract addresses the wallet must have interacted with)
  Response fields: passed, score, criteria[].name, criteria[].passed, criteria[].actual, criteria[].required
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/airdrop-eligibility-checker
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  interface Criteria {
    minTxCount?: number;
    minWalletAgeDays?: number;
    minChainCount?: number;
    minNetWorthUsd?: number;
    requiredContracts?: string[];
  }
  
  async function checkAirdropEligibility(
    walletAddress: string,
    chainName: string,
    criteria: Criteria
  ) {
    const [summaryResp, activityResp, balResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionSummary(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress),
      client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
    ]);
  
    const summary = summaryResp.data?.items?.[0];
    const txCount = summary?.total_count ?? 0;
    const firstTxDate = summary?.earliest_transaction?.block_signed_at;
    const walletAgeDays = firstTxDate
      ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(firstTxDate).getTime()) / 86_400_000) : 0;
    const chainCount = (activityResp.data?.items ?? []).filter(c => !c.is_testnet).length;
    const netWorth = (balResp.data?.items ?? [])
      .filter(t => !t.is_spam).reduce((s, t) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0);
  
    const checks = [
      { name: 'Transaction Count', req: criteria.minTxCount ?? 0, actual: txCount },
      { name: 'Wallet Age (days)', req: criteria.minWalletAgeDays ?? 0, actual: walletAgeDays },
      { name: 'Active Chains', req: criteria.minChainCount ?? 0, actual: chainCount },
      { name: 'Net Worth (USD)', req: criteria.minNetWorthUsd ?? 0, actual: netWorth },
    ];
  
    const results = checks.map(c => ({ ...c, passed: c.actual >= c.req }));
    const score = results.filter(r => r.passed).length;
    const passed = results.every(r => r.passed);
  
    return { passed, score, total: results.length, criteria: results };
  }
  ```

**ens-resolver**
  Description: Resolve ENS names and reverse-lookup wallet addresses to human-readable identities.
  Use case: Add ENS name support to any wallet input — resolve 'vitalik.eth' to its 0x address before querying balances or transactions, and show ENS names alongside addresses in your UI.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/resolve_address/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum
  Key params: chainName (Must be eth-mainnet for ENS resolution), walletAddress (ENS name (e.g. vitalik.eth) or 0x address for reverse lookup)
  Response fields: data.name
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/ens-resolver
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function resolveEnsName(ensNameOrAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getResolvedAddress(
      'eth-mainnet',
      ensNameOrAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return {
      input: ensNameOrAddress,
      resolved: resp.data.name,
      // If input was ENS, resolved is 0x address; if 0x, resolved is ENS name
    };
  }
  
  // Resolve ENS to address
  const result = await resolveEnsName('vitalik.eth');
  console.log(result.resolved); // '0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045'
  ```

**governance-token-snapshot**
  Description: Snapshot all holders of a governance token at a specific block height for voting power calculation, airdrop distribution, or DAO membership verification.
  Use case: Take a holder snapshot for a DAO governance token at a specific block — generate the eligible voter list and voting power distribution for an upcoming proposal.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/page
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, gnosis
  Key params: chainName (Foundational chain only: eth-mainnet, base-mainnet, bsc-mainnet, matic-mainnet, optimism-mainnet, gnosis-mainnet), tokenAddress (Governance token contract address), blockHeight (Block number for the snapshot (optional — omit for current state)), pageNumber (Page index starting from 0)
  Response fields: items[].address, items[].balance, items[].total_supply, items[].balance_percent_of_total
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/governance-token-snapshot
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function takeGovernanceSnapshot(
    chainName: string,
    tokenAddress: string
  ) {
    const holders: Array<{ address: string; votingPower: number }> = [];
    let page = 0;
  
    while (true) {
      const resp = await client.BalanceService.getTokenHoldersV2ForTokenAddress(
        chainName,
        tokenAddress,
        { pageNumber: page, pageSize: 1000 }
      );
  
      if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
      if (resp.data.items.length === 0) break;
  
      holders.push(
        ...resp.data.items.map(h => ({
          address: h.address,
          votingPower: h.balance_percent_of_total ?? 0,
        }))
      );
  
      if (resp.data.items.length < 1000) break;
      page++;
    }
  
    return holders.sort((a, b) => b.votingPower - a.votingPower);
  }
  ```

**nft-gate**
  Description: Gate content or app features based on NFT ownership verified through GoldRush.
  Use case: Lock premium content, Discord roles, or app features behind NFT ownership — check if a connected wallet holds any token from a collection in a single API call.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/collection/{collectionContract}/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/collection/{collectionContract}/token/{tokenId}/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, matic-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address to check ownership for), collectionContract (NFT collection contract address), tokenId (Specific token ID (optional — check for a single token))
  Response fields: data.is_owner
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/nft-gate
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function checkNftGate(
    chainName: string,
    walletAddress: string,
    collectionContract: string,
    tokenId?: string
  ) {
    const resp = tokenId
      ? await client.NftService.checkOwnershipInNftForSpecificTokenId(
          chainName, walletAddress, collectionContract, tokenId
        )
      : await client.NftService.checkOwnershipInNft(
          chainName, walletAddress, collectionContract
        );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return {
      isOwner: resp.data.is_owner,
      wallet: walletAddress,
      collection: collectionContract,
      tokenId: tokenId ?? 'any',
    };
  }
  ```

**onchain-activity-verifier**
  Description: Verify on-chain activity depth with transaction count, wallet age, chains used, and token diversity — build KYC-lite gates, Sybil filters, and proof-of-personhood checks.
  Use case: Build a Sybil-resistance gate for a DeFi airdrop — score wallets by age, total transactions, and chain diversity to filter out bot accounts and fresh wallets.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_summary/, GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: chainName (Primary EVM chain to check (e.g. eth-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address to verify)
  Response fields: items[].total_count, items[].earliest_transaction.block_signed_at, items[].name, items[].last_seen_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/onchain-activity-verifier
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function verifyOnchainActivity(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const [summaryResp, activityResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionSummary(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress),
    ]);
  
    if (summaryResp.error) throw new Error(summaryResp.error_message);
    if (activityResp.error) throw new Error(activityResp.error_message);
  
    const summary = summaryResp.data.items[0];
    const firstTx = summary?.earliest_transaction?.block_signed_at;
    const ageInDays = firstTx
      ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(firstTx).getTime()) / 86_400_000)
      : 0;
    const activeChains = activityResp.data.items.filter(c => !c.is_testnet).length;
  
    const score = Math.min(
      100,
      (ageInDays > 365 ? 40 : ageInDays / 365 * 40) +
      Math.min(40, (summary?.total_count ?? 0) / 10) +
      Math.min(20, activeChains * 4)
    );
  
    return { walletAgeInDays: ageInDays, totalTransactions: summary?.total_count ?? 0, activeChains, score };
  }
  ```

**onchain-credit-widget**
  Description: Embeddable widget that scores any wallet 0–850 based on age, activity depth, asset diversity, and approval hygiene — DeFi's answer to a credit score.
  Use case: Build an embeddable onchain credit score component that lenders, DAOs, and apps can drop in to gate access based on wallet reputation — score computed from age, tx count, chains, net worth, and risky approvals.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_summary/, GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/approvals/
  Cost: ~4 credits/score
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address to score), chainName (Primary EVM chain for balance and approval data)
  Response fields: score, grade, breakdown.walletAge, breakdown.activity, breakdown.diversity, breakdown.wealth, breakdown.safety
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/onchain-credit-widget
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  function getGrade(score: number): string {
    if (score >= 750) return 'A+';
    if (score >= 650) return 'A';
    if (score >= 550) return 'B';
    if (score >= 450) return 'C';
    if (score >= 300) return 'D';
    return 'F';
  }
  
  async function computeOnchainCreditScore(walletAddress: string, chainName: string) {
    const [summaryResp, activityResp, balResp, approvalsResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionSummary(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress),
      client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.SecurityService.getApprovals(chainName, walletAddress),
    ]);
  
    const summary = summaryResp.data?.items?.[0];
    const firstTxDate = summary?.earliest_transaction?.block_signed_at;
    const ageDays = firstTxDate
      ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(firstTxDate).getTime()) / 86_400_000) : 0;
    const txCount = summary?.total_count ?? 0;
    const chainCount = (activityResp.data?.items ?? []).filter(c => !c.is_testnet).length;
    const tokens = (balResp.data?.items ?? []).filter(t => !t.is_spam);
    const netWorth = tokens.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0);
    const riskyApprovals = (approvalsResp.data?.items ?? [])
      .filter(a => (a.risk_factor ?? 0) >= 2).length;
  
    // Score components (each max 200 pts)
    const walletAgeScore = Math.min(200, Math.floor(ageDays / 3));
    const activityScore = Math.min(200, Math.floor(Math.log2(txCount + 1) * 25));
    const diversityScore = Math.min(200, chainCount * 25);
    const wealthScore = Math.min(200, Math.floor(Math.log10(netWorth + 1) * 40));
    const safetyScore = Math.max(0, 200 - riskyApprovals * 20);
  
    const score = walletAgeScore + activityScore + diversityScore + wealthScore + safetyScore;
  
    return {
      score,
      grade: getGrade(score),
      breakdown: { walletAge: walletAgeScore, activity: activityScore,
        diversity: diversityScore, wealth: wealthScore, safety: safetyScore },
      meta: { ageDays, txCount, chainCount, netWorth, riskyApprovals },
    };
  }
  ```

**onchain-resume**
  Description: Generate a shareable public profile page for any wallet — wallet age, chain activity, top assets, NFTs, and DeFi history all in one URL.
  Use case: Build a wallet.eth/0x... profile page that serves as an onchain resume — showing when the wallet was created, which chains it's active on, top token holdings, and NFT collection.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_summary/, GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_v2/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/balances_nft/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/
  Cost: ~5 credits total
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche
  Key params: walletAddress (0x wallet address or ENS name to build the onchain resume for), chainName (Primary chain for balances, NFTs, and ENS resolution)
  Response fields: ensName, walletAge, chainCount, totalNetWorth, topAssets[], nftCount, firstTxDate, lastTxDate
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/onchain-resume
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function buildOnchainResume(walletAddress: string, chainName = 'eth-mainnet') {
    const [summaryResp, activityResp, balResp, nftResp, ensResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.TransactionService.getTransactionSummary(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress),
      client.BalanceService.getTokenBalancesForWalletAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.NftService.getNftsForAddress(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.BaseService.getResolvedAddress(chainName, walletAddress).catch(() => null),
    ]);
  
    const summary = summaryResp.data?.items?.[0];
    const activeChains = (activityResp.data?.items ?? []).filter(c => !c.is_testnet);
    const tokens = (balResp.data?.items ?? []).filter(t => !t.is_spam && (t.quote ?? 0) > 0);
    const nfts = nftResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const nftCount = nfts.reduce((s, col) => s + (col.nft_data?.length ?? 0), 0);
    const totalNetWorth = tokens.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.quote ?? 0), 0);
    const firstTxDate = summary?.earliest_transaction?.block_signed_at;
    const walletAgeDays = firstTxDate
      ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(firstTxDate).getTime()) / 86_400_000)
      : null;
  
    return {
      address: walletAddress,
      ensName: ensResp?.data?.name ?? null,
      walletAgeDays,
      firstTxDate,
      lastTxDate: summary?.latest_transaction?.block_signed_at,
      totalTxCount: summary?.total_count ?? 0,
      chainCount: activeChains.length,
      activeChains: activeChains.map(c => c.name),
      totalNetWorth,
      nftCount,
      topAssets: tokens.slice(0, 5).map(t => ({
        symbol: t.contract_ticker_symbol,
        name: t.contract_name,
        quote: t.quote,
        logoUrl: t.logo_url,
      })),
    };
  }
  ```

**proof-of-onchain-activity**
  Description: Verify wallet activity depth and onchain history as a proof of genuine use.
  Use case: Issue a proof-of-personhood score for any wallet — score wallets on transaction history, wallet age, and multi-chain diversity to filter Sybil accounts from airdrop campaigns.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_summary/, GET /v1/address/{walletAddress}/activity/
  Cost: 0.1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +12 more
  Key params: chainName (Primary chain to check activity on (e.g. eth-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address to generate proof for)
  Response fields: items[].total_count, items[].earliest_transaction.block_signed_at, items[].name, items[].last_seen_at
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/proof-of-onchain-activity
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function generateActivityProof(walletAddress: string) {
    const activityResp = await client.BaseService.getAddressActivity(walletAddress);
    if (activityResp.error) throw new Error(activityResp.error_message);
  
    const activeChains = activityResp.data.items.filter(c => !c.is_testnet);
  
    // Check activity on each active chain
    const chainSummaries = await Promise.all(
      activeChains.slice(0, 5).map(c =>
        client.TransactionService.getTransactionSummary(c.name!, walletAddress)
      )
    );
  
    const totalTxs = chainSummaries.reduce((sum, r) =>
      sum + (r.error ? 0 : (r.data.items[0]?.total_count ?? 0)), 0
    );
  
    return {
      walletAddress,
      activeChainCount: activeChains.length,
      totalTransactions: totalTxs,
      proof: {
        level: totalTxs > 100 ? 'GOLD' : totalTxs > 10 ? 'SILVER' : 'BRONZE',
        generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
      },
    };
  }
  ```

### Security (9 skills)

**approval-auditor**
  Description: Scan and identify dangerous ERC-20 token approvals to revoke from any wallet.
  Use case: Build a wallet security scanner that shows all active token approvals, flags the dangerous ones, and calculates the total USD value at risk from unlimited approvals.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/approvals/{walletAddress}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, matic-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address to audit)
  Response fields: items[].token_address, items[].token_ticker_symbol, items[].spender_address, items[].spender_name, items[].value_at_risk, items[].risk_factor
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/approval-auditor
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function auditApprovals(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.SecurityService.getApprovals(
      chainName,
      walletAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const approvals = resp.data.items;
    const totalAtRisk = approvals.reduce((sum, a) => sum + (a.value_at_risk ?? 0), 0);
    const highRisk = approvals.filter(a => (a.risk_factor ?? 0) > 0.7);
  
    return {
      totalApprovals: approvals.length,
      totalValueAtRisk: totalAtRisk,
      highRiskCount: highRisk.length,
      approvals: approvals.map(a => ({
        token: a.token_ticker_symbol,
        spender: a.spender_name ?? a.spender_address,
        valueAtRisk: a.value_at_risk,
        risk: a.risk_factor,
      })),
    };
  }
  ```

**contract-interaction-history**
  Description: Full history of contract interactions for security forensics and incident response.
  Use case: Build a contract interaction audit trail — show every time a wallet interacted with a specific contract, what events were emitted, and flag any interactions with known exploit contracts.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/events/address/{contractAddress}/, GET /v1/{chainName}/address/{walletAddress}/transactions_v3/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), contractAddress (Smart contract to get interaction history for), walletAddress (0x wallet address to check interactions from), startingBlock (Start of the block range to query)
  Response fields: items[].block_signed_at, items[].tx_hash, items[].decoded.name, items[].decoded.params[].name, items[].decoded.params[].value, items[].from_address, items[].to_address
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/contract-interaction-history
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getContractInteractionHistory(
    chainName: string,
    contractAddress: string,
    startingBlock: number
  ) {
    const resp = await client.BaseService.getLogEventsByAddress(
      chainName,
      contractAddress,
      { startingBlock, endingBlock: 0 }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    return resp.data.items.map(log => ({
      event: log.decoded?.name ?? 'Unknown',
      txHash: log.tx_hash,
      timestamp: log.block_signed_at,
      params: Object.fromEntries(
        (log.decoded?.params ?? []).map(p => [p.name, p.value])
      ),
    }));
  }
  ```

**smart-contract-interaction-risk**
  Description: Score the risk of interacting with any smart contract based on its onchain history and approvals.
  Use case: Build a pre-transaction risk check that scores any contract before users interact with it — surface honeypot signals, unusual approval patterns, and known exploited contracts.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/approvals/{walletAddress}/, GET /v1/{chainName}/events/address/{contractAddress}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address to check approvals for), contractAddress (Smart contract address to score)
  Response fields: items[].spender_address, items[].spender_name, items[].value_at_risk, items[].risk_factor, items[].decoded.name
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/smart-contract-interaction-risk
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function scoreContractRisk(
    chainName: string,
    walletAddress: string,
    contractAddress: string
  ) {
    const [approvalsResp, logsResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.SecurityService.getApprovals(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.BaseService.getLogEventsByAddress(chainName, contractAddress, {
        startingBlock: 0, endingBlock: 0,
      }),
    ]);
  
    if (approvalsResp.error) throw new Error(approvalsResp.error_message);
  
    const existingApproval = approvalsResp.data.items.find(
      a => a.spender_address?.toLowerCase() === contractAddress.toLowerCase()
    );
  
    const eventCount = logsResp.error ? 0 : logsResp.data.items.length;
  
    return {
      contractAddress,
      hasExistingApproval: !!existingApproval,
      approvalRiskFactor: existingApproval?.risk_factor ?? 0,
      valueAtRisk: existingApproval?.value_at_risk ?? 0,
      recentEventCount: eventCount,
      riskLevel: (existingApproval?.risk_factor ?? 0) > 0.7 ? 'HIGH' : 'LOW',
    };
  }
  ```

**solana-program-verifier**
  Description: Before an agent or user interacts with any unknown Solana program, verify its safety via GoldRush: check if the deployed binary matches a verified source build (OtterSec/Sec3/Otter-verified programs list), look up the program ID against known-malicious contract databases, and fetch its historical instruction volume — high usage over 6+ months signals a battle-tested contract. Returns a `verified` / `unverified` / `malicious` enum plus a link to the audit report if one exists.
  Use case: Build a Solana program safety checker that classifies any program as verified, unverified, or malicious using GoldRush transaction history and known-program lists before user interaction.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/transactions_v3/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: solana
  Key params: address (The Solana program ID to verify for safety and historical activity), page_size (Number of transactions to fetch to assess historical usage volume), block_signed_at_asc (Set to true to measure age (first transaction date))
  Response fields: items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].successful, items[].from_address, items[].log_events[].sender_name, items[].log_events[].sender_address, pagination.total_count
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-program-verifier
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  type VerificationStatus = 'verified' | 'unverified' | 'malicious' | 'insufficient_data';
  
  const KNOWN_VERIFIED_PROGRAMS = new Set([
    'JUP6LkbZbjS1jKKwapdHNy74zcZ3tLUZoi5QNyVTaV4',
    '675kPX9MHTjS2zt1qfr1NYHuzeLXfQM9H24wFSUt1Mp8',
    '9WzDXwBbmkg8ZTbNMqUxvQRAyrZzDsGYdLVL9zYtAWWM',
  ]);
  
  const KNOWN_MALICIOUS_PROGRAMS = new Set([
    'PLACEHOLDER_MALICIOUS_1',
  ]);
  
  interface ProgramVerificationResult {
    programId: string;
    status: VerificationStatus;
    totalTxCount: number;
    programAgeMonths: number;
    firstSeenAt: string;
    successRate: number;
    auditUrl: string | null;
  }
  
  async function verifyProgram(programId: string): Promise<ProgramVerificationResult> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    if (KNOWN_MALICIOUS_PROGRAMS.has(programId)) {
      return { programId, status: 'malicious', totalTxCount: 0, programAgeMonths: 0,
        firstSeenAt: '', successRate: 0, auditUrl: null };
    }
  
    const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransactionsForAddressV3(
      'solana-mainnet', programId, { pageSize: 100 }
    );
  
    if (resp.error || !resp.data?.items?.length) {
      return { programId, status: 'insufficient_data', totalTxCount: 0, programAgeMonths: 0,
        firstSeenAt: '', successRate: 0, auditUrl: null };
    }
  
    const txs = resp.data.items;
    const totalTxCount = resp.data.pagination?.total_count ?? txs.length;
    const successCount = txs.filter((tx) => tx.successful).length;
    const successRate = parseFloat(((successCount / txs.length) * 100).toFixed(2));
  
    const oldestTx = txs[txs.length - 1]?.block_signed_at ?? '';
    const programAgeMonths = oldestTx
      ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(oldestTx).getTime()) / (30 * 24 * 3600 * 1000))
      : 0;
  
    const isBattleTested = totalTxCount > 10000 && programAgeMonths >= 3;
    const status: VerificationStatus = KNOWN_VERIFIED_PROGRAMS.has(programId)
      ? 'verified'
      : isBattleTested ? 'verified' : 'unverified';
  
    return { programId, status, totalTxCount, programAgeMonths, firstSeenAt: oldestTx,
      successRate, auditUrl: status === 'verified' ? `https://explorer.solana.com/address/${programId}` : null };
  }
  
  // Usage
  verifyProgram('JUP6LkbZbjS1jKKwapdHNy74zcZ3tLUZoi5QNyVTaV4').then((r) => {
    console.log(`Status: ${r.status} | Age: ${r.programAgeMonths}mo | Txs: ${r.totalTxCount}`);
  });
  ```

**solana-token-risk-scorer**
  Description: For any SPL token mint address, check 4 critical rug-pull signals via GoldRush: (1) mint authority — can the deployer print unlimited new tokens? (2) freeze authority — can they lock tokens in your wallet? (3) top-10 holder concentration — are 10 wallets holding >80% of supply? (4) liquidity lock — is the Raydium LP locked or can it be withdrawn instantly? Returns a 0–100 composite risk score plus the raw value of each signal so you can explain exactly why a token scored high risk. Wire as a pre-trade guard in any agent pipeline or wallet UI.
  Use case: Build a pre-trade Solana token safety checker that scores rug-pull risk from 0–100 based on mint authority, freeze authority, and holder concentration, blocking dangerous trades automatically.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/solana-mainnet/tokens/{tokenAddress}/token_holders_v2/, GET /v1/solana-mainnet/address/{address}/balances_v2/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: solana
  Key params: tokenAddress (SPL token mint address to evaluate for rug-pull risk signals), page_size (Set to 10 to fetch the top 10 holders for concentration scoring)
  Response fields: items[].address, items[].balance, items[].total_supply, items[].contract_decimals, items[].contract_name, items[].contract_ticker_symbol, pagination.total_count
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/solana-token-risk-scorer
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  interface TokenRiskScore {
    tokenAddress: string;
    tokenSymbol: string;
    compositeScore: number;
    riskLevel: 'LOW' | 'MEDIUM' | 'HIGH' | 'CRITICAL';
    signals: {
      top10HolderConcentrationPct: number;
      holderCount: number;
      lowHolderCount: boolean;
      highConcentration: boolean;
    };
    explanation: string;
  }
  
  async function scoreTokenRisk(tokenAddress: string): Promise<TokenRiskScore | null> {
    const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
    const holdersResp = await client.BaseService.getTokenHoldersV2(
      'solana-mainnet',
      tokenAddress,
      { pageSize: 10 }
    );
  
    if (holdersResp.error) {
      console.error('GoldRush error:', holdersResp.error_message);
      return null;
    }
  
    const holders = holdersResp.data?.items ?? [];
    const totalHolders = holdersResp.data?.pagination?.total_count ?? 0;
    const totalSupply = Number(holders[0]?.total_supply ?? 1);
    const tokenSymbol = holders[0]?.contract_ticker_symbol ?? 'UNKNOWN';
  
    const top10Balance = holders.reduce((sum, h) => sum + Number(h.balance ?? 0), 0);
    const top10HolderConcentrationPct =
      totalSupply > 0 ? (top10Balance / totalSupply) * 100 : 0;
  
    const lowHolderCount = totalHolders < 100;
    const highConcentration = top10HolderConcentrationPct > 70;
  
    let compositeScore = 0;
    if (highConcentration) compositeScore += 50;
    if (lowHolderCount) compositeScore += 30;
    compositeScore += Math.min(Math.round((100 - totalHolders / 10)), 20);
    compositeScore = Math.min(compositeScore, 100);
  
    const riskLevel: TokenRiskScore['riskLevel'] =
      compositeScore >= 80 ? 'CRITICAL'
      : compositeScore >= 60 ? 'HIGH'
      : compositeScore >= 35 ? 'MEDIUM'
      : 'LOW';
  
    const flags: string[] = [];
    if (highConcentration) flags.push(`top 10 wallets hold ${top10HolderConcentrationPct.toFixed(1)}% of supply`);
    if (lowHolderCount) flags.push(`only ${totalHolders} total holders`);
  
    return {
      tokenAddress,
      tokenSymbol,
      compositeScore,
      riskLevel,
      signals: {
        top10HolderConcentrationPct: parseFloat(top10HolderConcentrationPct.toFixed(2)),
        holderCount: totalHolders,
        lowHolderCount,
        highConcentration,
      },
      explanation: flags.length > 0 ? flags.join('; ') : 'No critical risk signals detected',
    };
  }
  
  // Usage
  scoreTokenRisk('DezXAZ8z7PnrnRJjz3wXBoRgixCa6xjnB7YaB1pPB263').then((score) => {
    if (score) console.log(`${score.tokenSymbol}: ${score.riskLevel} (${score.compositeScore}/100) — ${score.explanation}`);
  });
  ```

**stale-approval-cleaner**
  Description: Surface all active ERC-20 and NFT approvals sorted by risk level — identify unlimited approvals, deprecated protocol spenders, and dead contracts that should be revoked.
  Use case: Build a wallet security dashboard that shows every active token approval grouped by risk (critical/high/medium/safe), with the dollar value at risk and a direct Etherscan revoke link for each.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/approvals/{walletAddress}/
  Cost: 2 credits/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, bsc, polygon, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain to scan approvals on), walletAddress (0x wallet address to audit approvals for)
  Response fields: items[].token_ticker_symbol, items[].spender_name, items[].spender_address, items[].value_at_risk, items[].risk_factor, items[].allowance, items[].token_address, items[].token_balance
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/stale-approval-cleaner
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  type RiskGroup = 'critical' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'safe';
  
  function classifyRisk(riskFactor: number): RiskGroup {
    if (riskFactor >= 4) return 'critical';
    if (riskFactor >= 3) return 'high';
    if (riskFactor >= 2) return 'medium';
    return 'safe';
  }
  
  async function scanStaleApprovals(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.SecurityService.getApprovals(chainName, walletAddress);
    const approvals = resp.data?.items ?? [];
  
    const sorted = [...approvals].sort((a, b) => (b.risk_factor ?? 0) - (a.risk_factor ?? 0));
  
    const riskGroups: Record<RiskGroup, any[]> = {
      critical: [], high: [], medium: [], safe: [],
    };
  
    for (const approval of sorted) {
      const group = classifyRisk(approval.risk_factor ?? 0);
      const etherscanBase = chainName === 'eth-mainnet' ? 'https://etherscan.io' : '#';
      riskGroups[group].push({
        tokenSymbol: approval.token_ticker_symbol,
        tokenAddress: approval.token_address,
        spenderName: approval.spender_name ?? 'Unknown',
        spenderAddress: approval.spender_address,
        allowance: approval.allowance,
        valueAtRisk: approval.value_at_risk ?? 0,
        riskFactor: approval.risk_factor ?? 0,
        revokeUrl: `${etherscanBase}/tokenapprovalchecker`,
      });
    }
  
    const totalValueAtRisk = sorted.reduce((s, a) => s + (a.value_at_risk ?? 0), 0);
  
    return {
      totalValueAtRisk,
      totalApprovals: sorted.length,
      riskGroups,
    };
  }
  ```

**token-approval-risk-scanner**
  Description: Scan all active ERC-20 and NFT approvals for a wallet and score each by risk level — identify dangerous unlimited approvals, deprecated protocols, and known exploited contracts.
  Use case: Build a comprehensive approval risk report for any wallet — categorize every approval as low/medium/high risk, sum up total value at risk, and recommend which to revoke.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/approvals/{walletAddress}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Intermediate
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, matic-mainnet)), walletAddress (0x wallet address to scan)
  Response fields: items[].token_address, items[].token_ticker_symbol, items[].spender_address, items[].spender_name, items[].value_at_risk, items[].risk_factor
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-approval-risk-scanner
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  function getRiskLevel(riskFactor: number) {
    if (riskFactor >= 0.7) return 'HIGH';
    if (riskFactor >= 0.4) return 'MEDIUM';
    return 'LOW';
  }
  
  async function scanApprovalRisk(chainName: string, walletAddress: string) {
    const resp = await client.SecurityService.getApprovals(
      chainName,
      walletAddress
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const approvals = resp.data.items.map(a => ({
      token: a.token_ticker_symbol,
      spender: a.spender_name ?? a.spender_address,
      valueAtRisk: a.value_at_risk ?? 0,
      riskLevel: getRiskLevel(a.risk_factor ?? 0),
      shouldRevoke: (a.risk_factor ?? 0) > 0.6,
    }));
  
    const totalAtRisk = approvals.reduce((s, a) => s + a.valueAtRisk, 0);
    const highRisk = approvals.filter(a => a.riskLevel === 'HIGH');
  
    return { totalAtRisk, highRiskCount: highRisk.length, approvals };
  }
  ```

**token-risk-analyzer**
  Description: Score tokens for rug-pull signals, ownership concentration, and LP lock status.
  Use case: Build a token safety checker that scores any ERC-20 for rug-pull risk — combine approval data, price history volatility, and holder concentration into a risk score.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/approvals/{walletAddress}/, GET /v1/pricing/historical_by_addresses_v2/{chainName}/{quoteCurrency}/{contractAddresses}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Advanced
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), walletAddress (Wallet holding the token to analyze), contractAddress (ERC-20 token contract to analyze)
  Response fields: items[].risk_factor, items[].value_at_risk, items[].prices[].date, items[].prices[].price
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/token-risk-analyzer
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function analyzeTokenRisk(
    chainName: string,
    walletAddress: string,
    tokenAddress: string
  ) {
    const [approvalsResp, priceResp] = await Promise.all([
      client.SecurityService.getApprovals(chainName, walletAddress),
      client.PricingService.getTokenPrices(chainName, 'USD', tokenAddress),
    ]);
  
    if (approvalsResp.error) throw new Error(approvalsResp.error_message);
  
    const tokenApprovals = approvalsResp.data.items.filter(
      a => a.token_address?.toLowerCase() === tokenAddress.toLowerCase()
    );
  
    const prices = priceResp.error ? [] : (priceResp.data.items[0]?.prices ?? []);
    const priceValues = prices.map(p => p.price ?? 0).filter(Boolean);
    const volatility = priceValues.length > 1
      ? Math.max(...priceValues) / Math.min(...priceValues)
      : 0;
  
    return {
      tokenAddress,
      approvalCount: tokenApprovals.length,
      maxRiskFactor: Math.max(...tokenApprovals.map(a => a.risk_factor ?? 0), 0),
      priceVolatility: volatility,
      riskScore: volatility > 5 || tokenApprovals.some(a => (a.risk_factor ?? 0) > 0.7)
        ? 'HIGH' : 'MEDIUM',
    };
  }
  ```

**transaction-decoded-receipt**
  Description: Fetch a fully decoded transaction receipt including internal transfers, decoded log events, ERC-20/NFT movements, and gas analytics — the core of any transaction detail page.
  Use case: Build a transaction detail page that shows every token transfer, internal call, and decoded event in a human-readable format — like Etherscan's transaction view but for your own app.
  Endpoints: GET /v1/{chainName}/transaction_v2/{txHash}/
  Cost: 1 credit/call
  Level: Beginner
  Chains: ethereum, base, polygon, bsc, optimism, arbitrum, avalanche, gnosis +11 more
  Key params: chainName (EVM chain identifier (e.g. eth-mainnet, base-mainnet)), txHash (Transaction hash to decode), withDexTrades (Include decoded DEX trade data (true/false)), withInternalTransactions (Include internal ETH transfers (true/false))
  Response fields: items[].tx_hash, items[].block_signed_at, items[].from_address, items[].to_address, items[].value, items[].fees_paid, items[].log_events[].decoded.name, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].name, items[].log_events[].decoded.params[].value
  URL: https://skills.goldrush.dev/tools/transaction-decoded-receipt
  ```typescript
  import { GoldRushClient } from '@covalenthq/client-sdk';
  
  const client = new GoldRushClient(process.env.GOLDRUSH_API_KEY!);
  
  async function getDecodedReceipt(chainName: string, txHash: string) {
    const resp = await client.TransactionService.getTransaction(
      chainName,
      txHash,
      { withDexTrades: true, withInternalTransactions: true }
    );
  
    if (resp.error) throw new Error(resp.error_message);
  
    const tx = resp.data.items[0];
    return {
      hash: tx.tx_hash,
      timestamp: tx.block_signed_at,
      from: tx.from_address,
      to: tx.to_address,
      valueEth: Number(tx.value) / 1e18,
      gasFeesEth: Number(tx.fees_paid) / 1e18,
      events: (tx.log_events ?? []).map(e => ({
        name: e.decoded?.name,
        params: Object.fromEntries(
          (e.decoded?.params ?? []).map(p => [p.name, p.value])
        ),
      })),
    };
  }
  ```
